entry.title: Re: WFA on HANA error
entry.content:
Hi @YSE-1 Thank you for looking into this.

I have tried updating the SQL but it is still failing.

First, you mentioned to remove the effective end date from the Job Info query but if I do that, the where clause fails because it uses it so I left it there.

Then, you mentioned to move the where clause right after the job info query, but that fails as well.

So I have just adjusted the division ID as you have mentioned.

Regardless, I do not think this is the root cause for the fail, I have checked the following, on Data Factory page I see this message under the Incremental Load: "TPTZ was changed, please reschedule, toggle if needed"

Then, troubleshooting the log for my build, the first error I get is this, which seems to be related to the message from Data Factory:

Creating function wfa_utc_to_tptz_nvarchar_ts (i_value nvarchar(5000) ) RETURNS e_res TIMESTAMP LANGUAGE SQLSCRIPT SQL SECURITY INVOKER AS BEGIN DECLARE ts TIMESTAMP; DECLARE EXIT HANDLER FOR SQL_ERROR_CODE 303 e_res = null; ts = CAST( i_value AS SECONDDATE); e_res = (CASE WHEN cast(i_value as timestamp) <= '0001-01-02' THEN '0001-01-01 00:00:00' WHEN cast(i_value as timestamp) >= '9999-12-30' THEN '9999-12-31 23:59:59.999999' ELSE UTCTOLOCAL( cast(i_value as timestamp) , 'Europe/Warsaw','platform') END); END;

Could you please support further?

Kind regards,

Alex

entry.title: Building a RAP Application with External SAP HANA Cloud using CDS External Entities – Part 2
entry.content:
Business scenario:

Many organizations already have business data stored in a separate SAP HANA Cloud database and want to build modern RAP (ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model) applications on SAP BTP ABAP Environment without replicating that data into the ABAP system.

The blog demonstrates this using a Loyalty Management application.

Scenario

  • Customer loyalty information (Memberships) is stored in an external SAP HANA Cloud database.
  • Transaction data related to each membership is also stored in the same external database.
  • The business wants a Fiori application where users can:
    • Create loyalty memberships
    • Maintain loyalty transactions
    • Update and delete records
    • View accumulated loyalty points
    • Persist all changes directly in the external HANA database

 


Prerequisites

Building a RAP Application with External SAP HANA Cloud using CDS External Entities – Part 1

Build CDS Data Model

For creating Loyalty Management Application, we need to have customer master data. For our reference scenario and to show only required fields, we will create a custom CDS entity over I_BusinessPartner .

@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'Value help for Business Partner'
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true

define view entity zlm_i_businesspartner_vh
  as select from I_BusinessPartner as BusinessPartner
{
      @ObjectModel.text.element: [ 'BusinessPartnerName' ]
      @Consumption.filter.hidden: true
  key BusinessPartner.BusinessPartner,
      @ui.hidden: true
      BusinessPartnerCategory,
      @EndUserText.label: 'Business Partner Name'
      @search.defaultSearchElement: true
      @search.fuzzinessThreshold: 0.8
      @search.ranking: #HIGH
      BusinessPartner.BusinessPartnerName

}

Ceate root entity (Membership) and child entity (Transactions).
Add composition:  One membership can have multiple transactions.
Use associations for linking data like Business Partner.

Create root entitity zlm_r_membship_ee

@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'CDS on Static EE for Loyalty Membership'
define root view entity zlm_r_membship_ee
  as select from zlm_membship_ee
  composition [1..*] of zlm_r_transctns_ee           as _MembershipTransactions
  association [1..1] to zlm_i_businesspartner_vh     as _BusinessPartner on $projection.Customer = _BusinessPartner.BusinessPartner
{
  key membershipuuid     as Membershipuuid,
      membershipid       as Membershipid,
      customer           as Customer,
      createdby          as Createdby,
      createdat          as Createdat,
      lastchangedby      as Lastchangedby,
      locallastchangedat as Locallastchangedat,
      lastchangedat      as Lastchangedat,
      _BusinessPartner,
      _MembershipTransactions
}
where
  zlm_membship_ee.client = $session.client

Save the cds zlm_r_membship_ee and don’t activate.
Create Child Entity zlm_r_transctns_ee.

@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'CDS on Static EE  for transactions'
define view entity zlm_r_transctns_ee
  as select from zlm_transctns_ee
  association to parent zlm_r_membship_ee as _LoyaltyMembership on $projection.Membershipuuid = _LoyaltyMembership.Membershipuuid
{
  key transactionuuid     as Transactionuuid,
      membershipuuid      as Membershipuuid,
      transactiondate     as Transactiondate,
      @Semantics.amount.currencyCode : 'transactioncurrency'
      transactionvalue    as Transactionvalue,
      transactioncurrency as Transactioncurrency,
      loyaltypoints       as Loyaltypoints,
      createdby           as Createdby,
      createdat           as Createdat,
      lastchangedby       as Lastchangedby,
      locallastchangedat  as Locallastchangedat,
      lastchangedat       as Lastchangedat,
      _LoyaltyMembership
}
where
  zlm_transctns_ee.client = $session.client

Save and activate both the entities using "Activate inactive development objects". 
To calculate the loyalty points at runtime along with draft mode, we create the following CDS view entities.

@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'Total Loyalty Points-Without draft'
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true
define view entity zlm_c_loyaltypoints_total_ee
  as select from zlm_r_transctns_ee
{
  key Membershipuuid,
      sum ( Loyaltypoints ) as Total
}
group by
  zlm_r_transctns_ee.Membershipuuid
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'Loyalty Pts in draft-existing Membership'
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true
define view entity zlm_c_loyaltypoints_dfttot_ee
  as select from zlm_trans_ee_d
{
  key membershipuuid,
      sum ( loyaltypoints ) as Total
}
group by
  zlm_trans_ee_d.membershipuuid
Merge the CDS views and compute the total loyalty points per membership.
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'Effective Loyalty Pts per Membership'
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true

define view entity zlm_c_membship_loyaltypts_ee
  as select from zlm_c_loyaltypoints_total_ee as actual
    left outer join zlm_c_loyaltypoints_dfttot_ee as draft
      on actual.Membershipuuid = draft.membershipuuid
{
  key actual.Membershipuuid               as Membershipuuid,
      coalesce (
        draft.Total,
        actual.Total
      )                                   as Total
}

union all

select from zlm_c_loyaltypoints_dfttot_ee as draft
  left outer join zlm_c_loyaltypoints_total_ee as actual
    on draft.membershipuuid = actual.Membershipuuid
{
  key draft.membershipuuid                as Membershipuuid,
      draft.Total                         as Total
}
where actual.Membershipuuid is null;

Define Behaviour

Enable CRUD operations:
- Create
- Update
- Delete
Add determination logic:
- Automatically calculate loyalty points when transaction value changes.

To perform CRUD operations, we need to define corresponding behaviour definitions and implementations for root and child entities.In ABAP Development Tools for eclipse, right click on zlm_r_membship_ee and create behaviour definition.

unmanaged implementation in class zbp_lm_membship_ee unique;
strict ( 2 );
with draft;
define behavior for zlm_r_membship_ee alias LoyaltyMembership
draft table zlm_memb_ee_d
lock master total etag Lastchangedat
authorization master ( instance )
etag master Locallastchangedat

{
  create ( precheck ,  authorization : global );
  update;
  delete;
  field ( numbering : managed ) Membershipuuid;
   field ( readonly ) Membershipuuid;
  draft action Activate optimized;
  draft action Discard;
  draft action Edit;
  draft action Resume;
  draft determine action Prepare;
  association _MembershipTransactions { create{default function GetDefaultsForCBA;} with draft; }
}

define behavior for zlm_r_transctns_ee alias MembershipTransactions
draft table zlm_trans_ee_d
lock dependent by _LoyaltyMembership
authorization dependent by _LoyaltyMembership
etag master Locallastchangedat
{
  update;
  delete;
  field ( numbering : managed ) Transactionuuid;
  field ( readonly ) Transactionuuid, Membershipuuid;
    //calculate loyalty points when a transaction is created or relevant fields are modified
  determination CalculateLoyaltyPoints on modify
  { create; field Transactionvalue; }
  side effects
  {
    field Transactionvalue affects field Loyaltypoints, entity _LoyaltyMembership;
  }
  association _LoyaltyMembership { with draft; }
}


Implement Business Logic

Create number range object ZLM_MID_EE via ADT and by using Fiori application “Manage Number range Intervals” , maintain the number range.

Create message class ZLM_MESSAGES_EE and add the following messages.

Create Interface zlm_if_constants_ee for maintaining constants.

INTERFACE zlm_if_constants_ee
  PUBLIC .
  CONSTANTS:
    nr_obj_value             TYPE cl_numberrange_runtime=>nr_object VALUE 'ZLM_MID_EE',
    nr_range_nr              TYPE cl_numberrange_runtime=>nr_interval VALUE '01',
    draft                    TYPE abp_behv_flag VALUE '00',
    cid                      TYPE abp_behv_cid VALUE '01'.
ENDINTERFACE.

Create class zcx_lm_messages_ee to handle exceptions in the application.

CLASS zcx_lm_messages_ee DEFINITION
  PUBLIC
  INHERITING FROM cx_static_check
  FINAL
  CREATE PUBLIC .
  PUBLIC SECTION.
    INTERFACES:
      if_t100_message,
      if_abap_behv_message.
    CONSTANTS:
      message_class TYPE symsgid VALUE 'ZLM_MESSAGES_EE',
      BEGIN OF extdb_cud_failure,
        msgid TYPE symsgid VALUE message_class,
        msgno TYPE symsgno VALUE '001',
        attr1 TYPE scx_attrname VALUE '',
        attr2 TYPE scx_attrname VALUE '',
        attr3 TYPE scx_attrname VALUE '',
        attr4 TYPE scx_attrname VALUE '',
      END OF extdb_cud_failure,
      BEGIN OF extdb_mcrt_success,
        msgid TYPE symsgid VALUE message_class,
        msgno TYPE symsgno VALUE '002',
        attr1 TYPE scx_attrname VALUE 'MV_MEMBERSHIPID',
        attr2 TYPE scx_attrname VALUE '',
        attr3 TYPE scx_attrname VALUE '',
        attr4 TYPE scx_attrname VALUE '',
      END OF extdb_mcrt_success,
      BEGIN OF extdb_mupd_success,
        msgid TYPE symsgid VALUE message_class,
        msgno TYPE symsgno VALUE '003',
        attr1 TYPE scx_attrname VALUE 'MV_MEMBERSHIPID',
        attr2 TYPE scx_attrname VALUE '',
        attr3 TYPE scx_attrname VALUE '',
        attr4 TYPE scx_attrname VALUE '',
      END OF extdb_mupd_success,
      BEGIN OF extdb_mdel_success,
        msgid TYPE symsgid VALUE message_class,
        msgno TYPE symsgno VALUE '004',
        attr1 TYPE scx_attrname VALUE 'MV_MEMBERSHIPID',
        attr2 TYPE scx_attrname VALUE '',
        attr3 TYPE scx_attrname VALUE '',
        attr4 TYPE scx_attrname VALUE '',
      END OF extdb_mdel_success,
      BEGIN OF extdb_tcrt_success,
        msgid TYPE symsgid VALUE message_class,
        msgno TYPE symsgno VALUE '005',
        attr1 TYPE scx_attrname VALUE 'MV_LOYALTYPOINTS',
        attr2 TYPE scx_attrname VALUE '',
        attr3 TYPE scx_attrname VALUE '',
        attr4 TYPE scx_attrname VALUE '',
      END OF extdb_tcrt_success,
      BEGIN OF extdb_tupd_success,
        msgid TYPE symsgid VALUE message_class,
        msgno TYPE symsgno VALUE '006',
        attr1 TYPE scx_attrname VALUE 'MV_LOYALTYPOINTS',
        attr2 TYPE scx_attrname VALUE '',
        attr3 TYPE scx_attrname VALUE '',
        attr4 TYPE scx_attrname VALUE '',
      END OF extdb_tupd_success,
      BEGIN OF extdb_tdel_success,
        msgid TYPE symsgid VALUE message_class,
        msgno TYPE symsgno VALUE '007',
        attr1 TYPE scx_attrname VALUE '',
        attr2 TYPE scx_attrname VALUE '',
        attr3 TYPE scx_attrname VALUE '',
        attr4 TYPE scx_attrname VALUE '',
      END OF extdb_tdel_success,
      BEGIN OF extdb_mdel_failure,
        msgid TYPE symsgid VALUE message_class,
        msgno TYPE symsgno VALUE '008',
        attr1 TYPE scx_attrname VALUE '',
        attr2 TYPE scx_attrname VALUE '',
        attr3 TYPE scx_attrname VALUE '',
        attr4 TYPE scx_attrname VALUE '',
      END OF extdb_mdel_failure.
    DATA:
      mv_membershipid  TYPE zlm_membershipid_ee,
      mv_loyaltypoints TYPE zlm_loyaltypoints_ee.
    METHODS:
      constructor
        IMPORTING
          textid        LIKE if_t100_message=>t100key         OPTIONAL
          previous      LIKE previous                         OPTIONAL
          severity      TYPE if_abap_behv_message=>t_severity DEFAULT  if_abap_behv_message=>severity-error
          membershipid  TYPE zlm_membershipid_ee   OPTIONAL
          loyaltypoints TYPE zlm_loyaltypoints_ee OPTIONAL
            PREFERRED PARAMETER textid .
  PROTECTED SECTION.
  PRIVATE SECTION.
ENDCLASS.
CLASS zcx_lm_messages_ee IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD constructor ##ADT_SUPPRESS_GENERATION.
    super->constructor( previous = previous ).
    me->if_abap_behv_message~m_severity = severity.
    me->mv_membershipid   = membershipid.
    me->mv_loyaltypoints  = loyaltypoints.
    CLEAR me->textid.
    IF textid IS INITIAL.
      if_t100_message~t100key = if_t100_message=>default_textid.
    ELSE.
      if_t100_message~t100key = textid.
    ENDIF.
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

Behaviour Implementation.

CLASS zbp_lm_membship_ee DEFINITION PUBLIC ABSTRACT FINAL FOR BEHAVIOR OF zlm_r_membship_ee.
ENDCLASS.
CLASS ZBP_LM_MEMBSHIP_EE IMPLEMENTATION.
ENDCLASS.

In the local types tab of class zbp_lm_membship_ee, add the following .

CLASS lcl_buffer DEFINITION CREATE PRIVATE.
  PUBLIC SECTION.
    TYPES: membship_crt TYPE TABLE FOR CREATE zlm_c_membship_ee.
    TYPES: transactns_crt TYPE TABLE FOR CREATE zlm_c_membship_ee\_membershiptransactions.
    TYPES: membship_upd TYPE TABLE FOR UPDATE zlm_c_membship_ee.
    TYPES: transactns_upd TYPE TABLE FOR UPDATE zlm_c_transctns_ee.
    TYPES: membship_del TYPE TABLE FOR DELETE zlm_c_membship_ee.
    TYPES: transactns_del TYPE TABLE FOR DELETE zlm_c_transctns_ee.
    CLASS-METHODS get_instance
      RETURNING VALUE(ro_instance) TYPE REF TO lcl_buffer.
    DATA create_memberships TYPE membship_crt READ-ONLY.
    DATA create_transactions TYPE transactns_crt READ-ONLY.
    DATA update_memberships TYPE membship_upd READ-ONLY.
    DATA update_transactions TYPE transactns_upd  READ-ONLY.
    DATA delete_memberships TYPE membship_del READ-ONLY.
    DATA delete_transactions TYPE transactns_del READ-ONLY.
    METHODS set_create_memberships   IMPORTING memberships TYPE membship_crt.
    METHODS set_create_transactions IMPORTING transactions TYPE transactns_crt.
    METHODS set_update_memberships   IMPORTING memberships TYPE membship_upd.
    METHODS set_update_transactions IMPORTING transactions TYPE transactns_upd.
    METHODS set_delete_memberships   IMPORTING memberships TYPE membship_del.
    METHODS set_delete_transactions IMPORTING transactions TYPE transactns_del.
  PRIVATE SECTION.
    CLASS-DATA: go_instance TYPE REF TO lcl_buffer.
ENDCLASS.
CLASS lcl_buffer IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD get_instance.
    IF go_instance IS NOT BOUND.
      go_instance = NEW #( ).
    ENDIF.
    ro_instance = go_instance.
  ENDMETHOD.
  METHOD set_create_memberships.
    me->create_memberships = CORRESPONDING #( memberships ).
  ENDMETHOD.
  METHOD set_create_transactions.
    me->create_transactions = CORRESPONDING #( transactions ) .
  ENDMETHOD.
  METHOD set_delete_memberships.
    me->delete_memberships = CORRESPONDING #( memberships ).
  ENDMETHOD.
  METHOD set_delete_transactions.
    me->delete_transactions = CORRESPONDING #( transactions ).
  ENDMETHOD.
  METHOD set_update_memberships.
    me->update_memberships = CORRESPONDING #( memberships ).
  ENDMETHOD.
  METHOD set_update_transactions.
    me->update_transactions = CORRESPONDING #( transactions ).
    LOOP AT me->update_transactions ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<upd_tran>).
      <upd_tran>-Lastchangedby = sy-uname.
      <upd_tran>-Lastchangedat = <upd_tran>-Locallastchangedat.
    ENDLOOP.
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.
CLASS lhc_loyaltymembership DEFINITION INHERITING FROM cl_abap_behavior_handler.
  PRIVATE SECTION.
    METHODS get_instance_authorizations FOR INSTANCE AUTHORIZATION
      IMPORTING keys REQUEST requested_authorizations FOR loyaltymembership RESULT result.
    METHODS get_global_authorizations FOR GLOBAL AUTHORIZATION
      IMPORTING REQUEST requested_authorizations FOR loyaltymembership RESULT result.
    METHODS create FOR MODIFY
      IMPORTING entities FOR CREATE loyaltymembership.
    METHODS update FOR MODIFY
      IMPORTING entities FOR UPDATE loyaltymembership.
    METHODS delete FOR MODIFY
      IMPORTING keys FOR DELETE loyaltymembership.
    METHODS read FOR READ
      IMPORTING keys FOR READ loyaltymembership RESULT result.
    METHODS lock FOR LOCK
      IMPORTING keys FOR LOCK loyaltymembership.
    METHODS rba_membershiptransactions FOR READ
      IMPORTING keys_rba FOR READ loyaltymembership\_membershiptransactions FULL result_requested RESULT result LINK association_links.
    METHODS cba_membershiptransactions FOR MODIFY
      IMPORTING entities_cba FOR CREATE loyaltymembership\_membershiptransactions.
    METHODS getdefaultsforcba FOR READ
      IMPORTING keys FOR FUNCTION loyaltymembership~getdefaultsforcba RESULT result.
    METHODS precheck_create FOR PRECHECK
      IMPORTING entities FOR CREATE loyaltymembership.
ENDCLASS.
CLASS lhc_loyaltymembership IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD get_instance_authorizations.
  ENDMETHOD.
  METHOD get_global_authorizations.
  ENDMETHOD.
  METHOD create.
    lcl_buffer=>get_instance( )->set_create_memberships( memberships = CORRESPONDING #( entities ) ).
    mapped-loyaltymembership = CORRESPONDING #( lcl_buffer=>get_instance( )->create_memberships ).
  ENDMETHOD.
  METHOD update.
    lcl_buffer=>get_instance( )->set_update_memberships( memberships = CORRESPONDING #( entities ) ).
    mapped-loyaltymembership = CORRESPONDING #( lcl_buffer=>get_instance( )->update_memberships ).
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD delete.
    lcl_buffer=>get_instance( )->set_delete_memberships( memberships = CORRESPONDING #( keys ) ).
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD read.
    DATA lylmem_uuids TYPE RANGE OF xsduuid_raw.
    lylmem_uuids = VALUE #( FOR key IN keys
                            LET s      = 'I'
                                o      = 'EQ'
                            IN  sign   = s
                                option = o
                            ( low = key-Membershipuuid ) ).
    SELECT
     FROM zlm_r_membship_ee
     FIELDS *
     WHERE "Clnt = @SY-mandt AND
           Membershipuuid IN @lylmem_uuids
     INTO TABLE @DATA(memberships) .
    result = CORRESPONDING #( memberships MAPPING  Membershipuuid = Membershipuuid
                                                   Membershipid = Membershipid
                                                   createdat = Createdat
                                                   createdby = Createdby
                                                   lastchangedat = Lastchangedat
                                                   lastchangedby = Lastchangedby
                                                   Locallastchangedat = Locallastchangedat ).
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD lock.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD rba_membershiptransactions.
    DATA lylmem_uuids TYPE RANGE OF xsduuid_raw.

    lylmem_uuids = VALUE #( FOR key IN keys_rba
                          LET s      = 'I'
                              o      = 'EQ'
                          IN  sign   = s
                              option = o
                          ( low = key-Membershipuuid ) ).
    SELECT
       FROM zlm_r_transctns_ee
       FIELDS *
       WHERE Membershipuuid IN @lylmem_uuids
       INTO TABLE @DATA(transactions) .
    result = CORRESPONDING #( transactions MAPPING Transactionuuid = Transactionuuid
                                                   Membershipuuid = Membershipuuid
                                                   Transactiondate = Transactiondate
                                                   Transactionvalue = Transactionvalue
                                                   Transactioncurrency = Transactioncurrency
                                                   Loyaltypoints = Loyaltypoints
                                                   createdat = Createdat
                                                   createdby = Createdby
                                                   lastchangedat = Lastchangedat
                                                   lastchangedby = Lastchangedby
                                                   Locallastchangedat = Locallastchangedat ).
  ENDMETHOD.
  METHOD cba_membershiptransactions.
    lcl_buffer=>get_instance( )->set_create_transactions( transactions = CORRESPONDING #( entities_cba MAPPING %target = %target  ) ).
    mapped-membershiptransactions = CORRESPONDING #( lcl_buffer=>get_instance( )->create_transactions ).
  ENDMETHOD.
  METHOD GetDefaultsForCBA.
    result = VALUE #( FOR key IN keys (
                        %tky = key-%tky
                        %param-Transactiondate = cl_abap_context_info=>get_system_date( )
                        %param-Transactioncurrency = 'INR'
                      ) ).
  ENDMETHOD.
  METHOD precheck_create.
    LOOP AT entities INTO DATA(ls_entity).
      "--------------------------------------------
      " Duplicate Check
      "--------------------------------------------
      SELECT SINGLE
      FROM zlm_R_membship_ee
      FIELDS Membershipid , Customer
      WHERE Customer = @LS_entity-Customer
      INTO @DATA(membership) .
      IF membership IS NOT INITIAL.
        APPEND VALUE #(
          %key = ls_entity-%key
          %msg = new_message(
                    id       = 'ZLM_MESSAGES_EE'
                    number   = '009'
                    v1       = membership-Membershipid
                    v2       = membership-Customer
                    severity = if_abap_behv_message=>severity-error )
        ) TO reported-loyaltymembership.

        APPEND VALUE #( %key = ls_entity-%key ) TO failed-loyaltymembership.

        CONTINUE.
      ENDIF.
      CLEAR membership.
    ENDLOOP.
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.
CLASS lhc_membershiptransactions DEFINITION INHERITING FROM cl_abap_behavior_handler.
  PRIVATE SECTION.
    METHODS update FOR MODIFY
      IMPORTING entities FOR UPDATE membershiptransactions.
    METHODS delete FOR MODIFY
      IMPORTING keys FOR DELETE membershiptransactions.
    METHODS read FOR READ
      IMPORTING keys FOR READ membershiptransactions RESULT result.
    METHODS rba_loyaltymembership FOR READ
      IMPORTING keys_rba FOR READ membershiptransactions\_loyaltymembership FULL result_requested RESULT result LINK association_links.
    METHODS calculateloyaltypoints FOR DETERMINE ON MODIFY
      IMPORTING keys FOR membershiptransactions~calculateloyaltypoints.
ENDCLASS.

CLASS lhc_membershiptransactions IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD update.
    lcl_buffer=>get_instance( )->set_update_transactions( transactions = CORRESPONDING #( entities ) ).
    mapped-membershiptransactions = CORRESPONDING #( lcl_buffer=>get_instance( )->update_transactions ).
  ENDMETHOD.
  METHOD delete.
    lcl_buffer=>get_instance( )->set_delete_transactions( transactions = CORRESPONDING #( keys ) ).
  ENDMETHOD.
  METHOD read.
  ENDMETHOD.
  METHOD rba_loyaltymembership.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD CalculateLoyaltyPoints.
    " Calculates loyalty points for a transaction based on its value, then updates the Loyaltypoints field in MembershipTransactions.
    DATA updateTransactions TYPE TABLE FOR UPDATE zlm_r_transctns_ee.
    " Read all relevant transaction instances.
    READ ENTITIES OF zlm_r_membship_ee IN LOCAL MODE
         ENTITY MembershipTransactions
         FIELDS ( Transactionuuid Membershipuuid Transactionvalue Loyaltypoints  )
         WITH CORRESPONDING #( keys )
      RESULT DATA(lylpointstransactions)
      FAILED DATA(failed).
    IF lylpointstransactions IS NOT INITIAL.
      lylpointstransactions[ 1 ]-Loyaltypoints =   lylpointstransactions[ 1 ]-Transactionvalue * '0.10' .
      updateTransactions  =  CORRESPONDING #( lylpointstransactions ).
      MODIFY ENTITIES OF zlm_r_membship_ee IN LOCAL MODE
                ENTITY MembershipTransactions
                UPDATE FIELDS ( Loyaltypoints )
                WITH updateTransactions
              MAPPED   DATA(updated_transactions)
              FAILED   DATA(update_failed)
              REPORTED  DATA(update_reported).
      IF reported IS NOT INITIAL OR update_failed IS NOT INITIAL.
*Fill reported
        reported = CORRESPONDING #( DEEP update_reported ).
*Set failed keys
        APPEND VALUE #(  %tky = lylpointstransactions[ 1 ]-%tky )
                      TO update_failed-membershiptransactions.
      ENDIF.
    ENDIF.
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.
CLASS lsc_zlm_r_membship_ee DEFINITION INHERITING FROM cl_abap_behavior_saver.
  PROTECTED SECTION.
    METHODS finalize REDEFINITION.
    METHODS check_before_save REDEFINITION.
    METHODS save REDEFINITION.
    METHODS cleanup REDEFINITION.
    METHODS cleanup_finalize REDEFINITION.
ENDCLASS.
CLASS lsc_zlm_r_membship_ee IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD finalize.
  ENDMETHOD.
  METHOD check_before_save.
  ENDMETHOD.
  METHOD save.
    DATA(create_memberships) = lcl_buffer=>get_instance( )->create_memberships.
    DATA(create_transactions) = lcl_buffer=>get_instance( )->create_transactions.
    DATA(update_memberships) = lcl_buffer=>get_instance( )->update_memberships.
    DATA(update_transactions) = lcl_buffer=>get_instance( )->update_transactions.
    DATA(delete_memberships) = lcl_buffer=>get_instance( )->delete_memberships.
    DATA(delete_transactions) = lcl_buffer=>get_instance( )->delete_transactions.

    IF create_memberships IS INITIAL
    AND create_transactions IS INITIAL
    AND update_memberships IS INITIAL
    AND update_transactions IS INITIAL
    AND delete_memberships IS INITIAL
    AND delete_transactions IS INITIAL.
      RETURN.
    ENDIF.
    TRY.

        FINAL(ext_con) = cl_abap_sql_connection_builder=>write_enabled_4_logical_schema(
          i_connection_name     = 'R/3*SERVICE'
          i_logical_schema_name = 'ZLM_EXT_SCHEMA_EE' )->create( ).

        IF create_memberships  IS NOT INITIAL.
          " Request sequential numbers from Number Range Object
          TRY.
              cl_numberrange_runtime=>number_get( EXPORTING nr_range_nr       = zlm_if_constants_ee=>nr_range_nr
                                                            object            = zlm_if_constants_ee=>nr_obj_value
                                                            quantity          = CONV #( lines( create_memberships ) )
                                                  IMPORTING number            = DATA(number_range_key) ).
            CATCH cx_number_ranges INTO DATA(nr_allocation_failed).
              INSERT VALUE #( %msg = new_message_with_text( severity = if_abap_behv_message=>severity-error
                                                              text     = nr_allocation_failed->get_text( ) ) )
                     INTO TABLE reported-loyaltymembership.
              EXIT.
          ENDTRY.
          INSERT zlm_membship_cud_ee PROVIDED BY zlm_ext_schema_ee CONNECTION @ext_con
             FROM TABLE  @( VALUE #( FOR memb IN create_memberships
                                     (
                                     client                = sy-mandt
                                     membershipuuid        = memb-membershipuuid
                                     membershipid          = number_range_key "memb-membershipid
                                     customer              = memb-customer
                                     createdby            = memb-createdby
                                     createdat            = memb-createdat
                                     lastchangedby       = memb-lastchangedby
                                     lastchangedat       = memb-lastchangedat
                                     locallastchangedat = memb-locallastchangedat
                                   ) ) ).
          IF sy-subrc = 0.
            reported-loyaltymembership = CORRESPONDING #( create_memberships ).
            LOOP AT reported-loyaltymembership ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<mem>).
              DATA(membershipuuid) = <mem>-membershipuuid .
              <mem>-%msg = NEW zcx_lm_messages_ee(
                textid     = zcx_lm_messages_ee=>extdb_mcrt_success
                severity   = if_abap_behv_message=>severity-success
                membershipid = CONV #( number_range_key ) )."create_memberships[ KEY entity COMPONENTS Membershipuuid = <mem>-membershipuuid ]-Membershipid ).
            ENDLOOP.
          ELSE.
            reported-%other = VALUE #( ( NEW zcx_lm_messages_ee(
                                       textid   = zcx_lm_messages_ee=>extdb_cud_failure
                                       severity = if_abap_behv_message=>severity-error ) ) ).
            RAISE EXCEPTION TYPE zcx_lm_messages_ee.
          ENDIF.

        ENDIF.
        IF update_memberships  IS NOT INITIAL.
          UPDATE zlm_membship_cud_ee PROVIDED BY zlm_ext_schema_ee CONNECTION @ext_con
             FROM TABLE  @( VALUE #( FOR memb_upd IN update_memberships
                                     (
                                     lastchangedby       = memb_upd-lastchangedby
                                     lastchangedat       = memb_upd-lastchangedat
                                     locallastchangedat = memb_upd-locallastchangedat
                                   ) ) ).


          IF sy-subrc = 0.
            reported-loyaltymembership = CORRESPONDING #( update_memberships ).
            LOOP AT reported-loyaltymembership ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<mem_upd>).
              membershipuuid = <mem_upd>-membershipuuid.
              <mem_upd>-%msg = NEW zcx_lm_messages_ee(
                textid     = zcx_lm_messages_ee=>extdb_mupd_success
                severity   = if_abap_behv_message=>severity-success
                membershipid = update_memberships[  KEY entity membershipuuid = <mem_upd>-membershipuuid ]-membershipid ).
            ENDLOOP.
          ELSE.
            reported-%other = VALUE #( ( NEW zcx_lm_messages_ee(
                                       textid   = zcx_lm_messages_ee=>extdb_cud_failure
                                       severity = if_abap_behv_message=>severity-error ) ) ).
            RAISE EXCEPTION TYPE zcx_lm_messages_ee.            .
          ENDIF.
        ENDIF.


        IF delete_memberships  IS NOT INITIAL.

          DELETE zlm_membship_cud_ee PROVIDED BY zlm_ext_schema_ee CONNECTION @ext_con
             FROM TABLE  @( VALUE #( FOR memb_del IN delete_memberships
                                     (
                                     client = sy-mandt
                                     membershipuuid        = memb_del-membershipuuid
                                   )  ) ).
          IF sy-subrc = 0.
            reported-loyaltymembership = CORRESPONDING #( delete_memberships ).
            LOOP AT reported-loyaltymembership ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<mem_del>).
              <mem_del>-%msg = NEW zcx_lm_messages_ee(
                textid   = zcx_lm_messages_ee=>extdb_mdel_success
                severity = if_abap_behv_message=>severity-success ).
            ENDLOOP.
          ELSE.
            reported-%other = VALUE #( ( NEW zcx_lm_messages_ee(
                                       textid   = zcx_lm_messages_ee=>extdb_cud_failure
                                       severity = if_abap_behv_message=>severity-error ) ) ).
            RAISE EXCEPTION TYPE zcx_lm_messages_ee.
          ENDIF.

        ENDIF.
        IF create_transactions  IS NOT INITIAL.
          INSERT zlm_transctns_cud_ee PROVIDED BY zlm_ext_schema_ee CONNECTION @ext_con
             FROM TABLE  @( VALUE #( FOR txns IN create_transactions
                                     FOR txn IN txns-%target
                                     (
                                      client          = sy-mandt
                                      transactionuuid = txn-transactionuuid
                                      membershipuuid =  txn-membershipuuid
                                      transactiondate = txn-transactiondate
                                      transactionvalue = txn-transactionvalue
                                      transactioncurrency = txn-transactioncurrency
                                      loyaltypoints = txn-loyaltypoints
                                      createdby = txn-createdby
                                      createdat = txn-createdat
                                      lastchangedby = txn-lastchangedby
                                      locallastchangedat = txn-locallastchangedat
                                      lastchangedat = txn-lastchangedat
                                ) ) ).

          IF sy-subrc = 0.
            LOOP AT create_transactions ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<txns>).
              LOOP AT <txns>-%target ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<txn>).
                membershipuuid = <txn>-membershipuuid.
                reported-membershiptransactions = VALUE #( BASE reported-membershiptransactions
                                                        (

                                                     Transactionuuid = <txn>-Transactionuuid
                                                      %msg       = NEW zcx_lm_messages_ee(
                                                      textid     = zcx_lm_messages_ee=>extdb_tcrt_success
                                                      severity   = if_abap_behv_message=>severity-success
                                                      loyaltypoints = <txn>-Loyaltypoints

                                                      ) ) ).
              ENDLOOP.
            ENDLOOP.

          ELSE.
            reported-%other = VALUE #( ( NEW zcx_lm_messages_ee(
                                       textid   = zcx_lm_messages_ee=>extdb_cud_failure
                                       severity = if_abap_behv_message=>severity-error ) ) ).
            RAISE EXCEPTION TYPE zcx_lm_messages_ee.
          ENDIF.
        ENDIF.

        IF update_transactions  IS NOT INITIAL.
          UPDATE zlm_transctns_cud_ee PROVIDED BY zlm_ext_schema_ee CONNECTION @ext_con
             FROM TABLE  @( VALUE #( FOR txns_upd IN update_transactions
                                   (
                                      client          = sy-mandt
                                      transactionuuid = txns_upd-transactionuuid
                                      membershipuuid =  txns_upd-membershipuuid
                                      transactiondate = txns_upd-transactiondate
                                      transactionvalue = txns_upd-transactionvalue
                                      transactioncurrency = txns_upd-transactioncurrency
                                      loyaltypoints = txns_upd-loyaltypoints
                                      createdby = txns_upd-createdby
                                      createdat = txns_upd-createdat
                                      lastchangedby = txns_upd-lastchangedby
                                      locallastchangedat = txns_upd-locallastchangedat
                                      lastchangedat = txns_upd-lastchangedat
                                   ) ) ).

          IF sy-subrc = 0.

            LOOP AT update_transactions ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<txn_upd>).
              reported-membershiptransactions = VALUE #( BASE reported-membershiptransactions
                                                      (
                                                      Transactionuuid = <txn_upd>-Transactionuuid
                                                      %msg       = NEW zcx_lm_messages_ee(
                                                      textid     = zcx_lm_messages_ee=>extdb_tupd_success
                                                      severity   = if_abap_behv_message=>severity-success
                                                      loyaltypoints = <txn_upd>-Loyaltypoints
                                                       ) ) ).
            ENDLOOP.
          ELSE.
            reported-%other = VALUE #( ( NEW zcx_lm_messages_ee(
                                       textid   = zcx_lm_messages_ee=>extdb_cud_failure
                                       severity = if_abap_behv_message=>severity-error ) ) ).
            RAISE EXCEPTION TYPE zcx_lm_messages_ee.
          ENDIF.
        ENDIF.
        IF delete_transactions  IS NOT INITIAL.
          DELETE zlm_transctns_cud_ee PROVIDED BY zlm_ext_schema_ee CONNECTION @ext_con
             FROM TABLE   @( VALUE #( FOR trans_del IN delete_transactions
                                     (
                                     client = sy-mandt
                                     transactionuuid = trans_del-transactionuuid
                                   )  ) ).
          IF sy-subrc = 0.
            LOOP AT delete_transactions ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<txn_del>).
              reported-membershiptransactions = VALUE #( BASE reported-membershiptransactions
                                                      ( Transactionuuid = <txn_del>-Transactionuuid
                                                        %msg       = NEW zcx_lm_messages_ee(
                                                        textid   = zcx_lm_messages_ee=>extdb_tdel_success
                                                        severity = if_abap_behv_message=>severity-success
                                                    ) ) ).
            ENDLOOP.
          ELSE.
            reported-%other = VALUE #( ( NEW zcx_lm_messages_ee(
                                       textid   = zcx_lm_messages_ee=>extdb_cud_failure
                                       severity = if_abap_behv_message=>severity-error ) ) ).
            RAISE EXCEPTION TYPE zcx_lm_messages_ee.
          ENDIF.
        ENDIF.
        COMMIT CONNECTION @ext_con.
        ext_con->close( ).
      CATCH cx_root INTO FINAL(exc).
        APPEND VALUE #(
                %msg = new_message_with_text(
                         severity = if_abap_behv_message=>severity-error
                         text     = exc->get_text( ) ) )
                TO reported-loyaltymembership.
    ENDTRY.
    READ ENTITIES OF zlm_r_membship_ee IN LOCAL MODE ENTITY LoyaltyMembership
    ALL FIELDS
    WITH VALUE #( ( membershipuuid = membershipuuid ) )
    RESULT DATA(loyaltymembership).
  ENDMETHOD.
  METHOD cleanup.
  ENDMETHOD.
  METHOD cleanup_finalize.
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

Projection Layer

Create projection views for UI consumption

Purpose:

- Simplify backend data model

- Expose only required fields

We now create the corresponding consumption view zlm_c_membship_ee .

@Metadata.allowExtensions: true
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'Projection View'
define root view entity zlm_c_membship_ee
  provider contract transactional_query
  as projection on zlm_r_membship_ee

{
  key Membershipuuid,
      Membershipid,
      Customer,
      _BusinessPartner.BusinessPartnerName as CustomerName,
      _Loyaltypoints.total                 as Loyaltypoints,
      Createdby,
      Createdat,
      Lastchangedby,
      Locallastchangedat,
      Lastchangedat,
      /* Associations */
      _MembershipTransactions : redirected to composition child zlm_c_transctns_ee
}

Save the cds entity zlm_c_membship_ee  and don’t activate.

Create consumption view zlm_c_transctns_ee.

@Metadata.allowExtensions: true
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'Projection View'
define view entity zlm_c_transctns_ee
  as projection on zlm_r_transctns_ee
{
  key Transactionuuid,
      Membershipuuid,
      Transactiondate,
      @Semantics.amount.currencyCode : 'Transactioncurrency'
      Transactionvalue,
      @Consumption.valueHelpDefinition: [{
      entity: {
         name: 'I_Currency',
         element: 'Currency'
      },
      useForValidation: true
      }]
      Transactioncurrency,
      Loyaltypoints,
      Createdby,
      Createdat,
      Lastchangedby,
      Locallastchangedat,
      Lastchangedat,
      /* Associations */
      _LoyaltyMembership : redirected to parent zlm_c_membship_ee // Make association public
}

Save and activate both the entities using “Activate inactive ABAP development objects”.

We now create behaviour projection zlm_c_membship_ee .

projection;
strict ( 2 );
use draft;
use side effects;

define behavior for zlm_c_membship_ee alias LoyaltyMembership
{
  use create;
  use update;
  use delete;

  field ( readonly ) Membershipuuid,Membershipid;
  field (readonly:update) Customer;
  use action Activate;
  use action Discard;
  use action Edit ;
  use action Resume;
  use action Prepare;

  //use association _MembershipTransactions { create; with draft;}
  use association _MembershipTransactions { create ; with draft; }
  use function GetDefaultsForCBA;

}

define behavior for zlm_c_transctns_ee alias MembershipTransactions
{
  use update;
  use delete;
  field ( readonly ) Loyaltypoints;
  use association _LoyaltyMembership { with draft;}
}

UI Annotations

Add annotations:
- @ui.lineItem → table columns
- @ui.identification → object page fields
- @Consumption.valueHelp → dropdown values

These control Fiori UI behavior.
Create Metadata extension for zlm_c_membship_ee .

@Metadata.layer: #CORE
@Search.searchable: true
@UI: {
  headerInfo: {
                typeName: 'Loyalty Management',
                typeNamePlural: 'Loyalty Management ',
                title: { type: #STANDARD, label: 'Loyalty Management', value: 'Membershipid' }},
  presentationVariant: [{ sortOrder: [{ by: 'Membershipid', direction:  #ASC }] }] }
annotate view zlm_c_membship_ee with
{

  @ui.facet: [
      {
        id: 'LoyaltyMembership',
        purpose: #STANDARD,
        label: 'Membership Details',
        type: #IDENTIFICATION_REFERENCE,
        position: 10
      },
     {
        id: 'Transactions',
        label: 'Transactions',
        type: #LINEITEM_REFERENCE,
        position: 20,
        targetElement: '_MembershipTransactions'
     }]

  @ui.hidden: true
  Membershipuuid;
  @ui: {  identification  : [ { position: 10, label: 'Membership ID' } ],
          lineItem        : [ { position: 10, label: 'Membership ID' }]
       }
  Membershipid;


  @ui: {  identification  : [ { position: 20, label: 'Customer ID' } ],
        lineItem        : [ { position: 20, label: 'Customer ID' } ] }
  @Consumption.valueHelpDefinition: [{  entity: {   name: 'zlm_i_businesspartner_vh' ,
                                                         element: 'BusinessPartner'  }     }]
  Customer;
  @ui: {  identification  : [ { position: 30, label: 'Customer Name' } ],
        lineItem        : [ { position: 30, label: 'Customer Name' } ],
        selectionField : [ { position: 30 } ]
     }
  @search.defaultSearchElement: true

  CustomerName;

  @ui: {  identification  : [ { position: 40, label: 'Loyalty Points' } ],
       lineItem        : [ { position: 40, label: 'Loyalty Points' } ],
       selectionField : [ { position: 40 } ]
    }
  Loyaltypoints;

  @ui.hidden: true
  Createdby;

  @ui.hidden: true
  Createdat;

  @ui.hidden: true
  Lastchangedby;

  @ui.hidden: true
  Locallastchangedat;

  @ui.hidden: true
  Lastchangedat;

}


Metadata extension for zlm_c_transctns_ee.

@Metadata.layer: #CORE
@UI: {
  headerInfo: { typeName: 'Loyalty Membership Transaction',
                typeNamePlural: 'Loyalty Membership Transactions',
                title: { type: #STANDARD, label: 'Loyalty Membership Transactions', value: 'Transactiondate' } } }
annotate entity zlm_c_transctns_ee with
{
  @ui.facet: [
     {
      id: 'Transactions',
      purpose: #STANDARD,
      label: 'Transactions',
      type: #IDENTIFICATION_REFERENCE
     }
   ]

  @ui.hidden: true
  Transactionuuid;
  @ui.hidden: true
  Membershipuuid;
  @ui.identification: [ {
    position: 10 ,
    label: 'Transaction date'
  } ]
  @ui.lineItem: [ {
    position: 10 ,
    label: 'Transaction date'
  } ]
  @ui.selectionField: [ {
    position: 10
  } ]
  Transactiondate;
  @ui.identification: [ {
      position: 20 ,
      label: 'Transaction value'
    } ]
  @ui.lineItem: [ {
    position: 20 ,
    label: 'Transaction value'
  } ]
  @ui.selectionField: [ {
    position: 20
  } ]
  Transactionvalue;
  @ui.identification: [ {
    position: 30 ,
    label: 'Loyalty points'
  } ]
  @ui.lineItem: [ {
    position: 30 ,
    label: 'Loyalty points'
  } ]
  @ui.selectionField: [ {
    position: 30
  } ]
  Loyaltypoints;
  @ui.hidden: true
  Createdby;
  @ui.hidden: true
  Createdat;
  @ui.hidden: true
  Lastchangedby;
  @ui.hidden: true
  Locallastchangedat;
  @ui.hidden: true
  Lastchangedat;
}

Service Definition & Binding

Create OData V4 service:
- Expose projection views
- Create service binding
- Publish service

This enables UI consumption.

Create service definition and service binding.

@EndUserText.label: 'OData V4 UI loyalty membership'
define service ZLY_UI_MEMBERSHIP_EE_O4 {
  expose zlm_c_membship_ee  as LoyaltyMembership;
  expose zlm_c_transctns_ee as MembershipTransactions;
}

 

Testing

  1. Open binding
  2. Launch Fiori app
  3. Create membership
  4.  Add transaction
  5.  Verify loyalty points calculation
  6. Check that data is saved in external HANA database.

End-to-End Flow

  1. User enters data in UI
  2. RAP behavior triggers
  3. Data stored temporarily in buffer
  4. Save method writes to external DB
  5. Commit completes transaction
  6. UI displays updated data

Key Takeaways.

  1. RAP model separates data, behavior, and UI
  2. Logical schema handles secure connectivity
  3. Business logic is implemented in ABAP classes
  4. No data replication required

Further info

  • External Entities | SAP Help Portal
  • External Entities (ABAP Keyword Documentation)
  • Working with Logical External Schemas | SAP Help Portal
  • Working with Outbound Services | SAP Help Portal
  • Communication Management | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud Appliance Library client export/import SCCX
entry.content:
HI @boyan_ilievf85ab3 
I hope you are well. Yeah SCC9N works fine for remote client copy onS/4HANA 2025the trode itself is not 
restricted by the fully activated application .
The real prerequisite having your RFC connection setup correctly in SM59 before yo run it, and making sure both systems are on compatible support package levels  or the copy throws the mismatch errors .
One thing from experience run it in background rather than dialog since remote copies on S/4 tend to run long and time out in foreground.

entry.title: Why Stock Fields Return Zero in Custom CDS Views on MARC - and How to Resolve It
entry.content:
Why Stock Fields Return Zero in Custom CDS Views on MARC - and How to Resolve It

I was building a custom CDS view on the MARC table (Plant Data for Material) to extract data into SAP Datasphere using Change Data Capture (CDC) for delta loads.

After activating the view, I checked the data and found something odd. The field TRAME (Stock in Transit) had a proper value in the MARC table, but in my CDS view the same field was completely empty.

To give a concrete example, for one material and plant the MARC table showed TRAME = 50.000, but my CDS view showed TRAME = 0.000 for the same key. All the master data fields were perfectly fine. Only the stock field was wrong.

That single clue — master data correct, stock field zero — is what pointed me to the real cause.


Where the data actually lives in S/4HANA

In the older SAP ECC system, stock quantities were physically saved in many tables, and the same figure was often repeated in several places. This created redundancy and slowed things down.

In S/4HANA, SAP redesigned this part of the system with the New Simplified Data Model (NSDM). The idea is straightforward: all stock movements are written into a single table called MATDOC, and the stock figures are calculated when you ask for them instead of being stored everywhere.

Because of this change, MARC became a so-called hybrid table. It still physically stores the material master and planning data, but the stock quantity fields are no longer filled in the table itself. Fields like TRAME, UMLMC, GLGMG and BWESB are now worked out on the fly from MATDOC whenever they are read.

So in S/4HANA, the TRAME column inside the physical MARC table is basically empty. The real stock-in-transit figure is calculated from MATDOC only at the moment it is needed.

Why SE16 showed the value but my CDS did not

This is the most important point of the whole story. SE16 and my CDS view were reading the same table through two completely different paths.

When you open MARC in SE16, the read goes through the normal application layer. SAP automatically redirects that read to a proxy object behind MARC, which performs the MATDOC calculation and returns the correct TRAME value. That is why SE16 happily showed 50.000.

My CDS view, on the other hand, was written as select from marc. A CDS view runs straight on the database and reads the physical MARC table directly. It never passes through the redirect, so it picked up the empty physical column and returned 0.000.

In one sentence:

SE16 reads MARC through the proxy and gets the calculated value. A CDS that selects directly from MARC reads the raw table and gets nothing. That difference in the read path was the entire reason the data "did not come."


My first fix — and why it failed

My first idea was simple. If the proxy returns the correct value, why not read from the proxy instead of the table? So I changed the source of my view to the proxy object:

Before (TRAME comes as zero):

@AbapCatalog.sqlViewName: 'ZCDS_MARC_DEX'

@AbapCatalog.compiler.compareFilter: true

@AbapCatalog.preserveKey: true

@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED

@EndUserText.label: 'Plant Data for Material'

@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true

@analytics.dataExtraction:{enabled: true,

delta.changeDataCapture:{mapping:[

{table:'marc',role:#MAIN,

viewElement:['Matnr','Werks'],

tableElement:['matnr','werks']}]}}

define view ZCDS_MARC_DEX_CDC as select from marc

{

   key matnr as Matnr,

   key werks as Werks,

   pstat as Pstat,

   lvorm as Lvorm,

   bwtty as Bwtty,

   xchar as Xchar,

   trame as Trame,

... remaining MARC fields ...

After (TRAME correct and CDC activates):

@AbapCatalog.sqlViewName: 'ZCDS_MARC_DEX'

@AbapCatalog.compiler.compareFilter: true

@AbapCatalog.preserveKey: true

@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED

@EndUserText.label: 'Plant Data for Material'

@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true 

@analytics:{

dataExtraction.enabled: true,

dataExtraction.delta.changeDataCapture:{

mapping:[

   {

      table:'marc',

      role:#MAIN,

      viewElement:['Matnr','Werks'],

      tableElement:['matnr','werks']

   }]

}

}

define view ZCDS_MARC_DEX_CDC as select from nsdm_e_marc

{

   key matnr as Matnr,

   key werks as Werks,

   pstat as Pstat,

   lvorm as Lvorm,

   bwtty as Bwtty, 

   xchar as Xchar,

   trame as Trame,

... remaining MARC fields ...

The only real change is the data source: from marc to nsdm_e_marc. The CDC mapping still refers to the physical table marc


This is not only a MARC problem

The same behaviour happens with all NSDM stock tables in S/4HANA. If your CDS reads stock fields straight from any of these, you can get zeros:

  • MARC – plant stock fields (TRAME, UMLMC, GLGMG, BWESB)
  • MARD – storage location stock
  • MCHB – batch stock
  • MKOL – special / consignment stock
  • MSKA – sales order stock
  • MSKU – customer special stock
  • MSLB – stock with vendor (subcontracting)
  • MSPR, MSSA, MSSQ – project and sales order stock totals
  • MSTB, MSTE, MSTQ – stock in transit tables


A simple rule of thumb: if a table held stock quantities in ECC, in S/4HANA those quantities are now calculated, and a direct CDS read will return zero. Use the table's proxy instead.

How to find the correct proxy in your own system

You do not have to guess the name. For any table (example: MARC):

  1. Open the table in SE11.
  2. Choose menu Extras → Replacement Object to see the assigned proxy (for MARC it is NSDM_E_MARC).

Example – "MARC" Replacement Object

Conclusion

What first looked like a bug in my CDS view turned out to be normal S/4HANA behaviour. Under the New Simplified Data Model, stock quantity fields are no longer stored in tables like MARC — they are calculated from MATDOC only when they are read. SE16 shows the correct value because it goes through the replacement object, while a CDS view that reads the table directly picks up the empty physical column and returns zero.

The fix itself is small but important: source the CDS view from the replacement object (for MARC, NSDM_E_MARC) instead of the base table. After that one change, TRAME returned the correct value. And because this is standard NSDM behaviour, the same approach works for every stock table in the family — whenever a stock field comes back as zero, the first thing to check is whether you are reading the base table directly instead of its replacement object.

The bigger lesson I took away is to always confirm where a field's data actually lives before building a CDS view on top of a table. A quick look at SE11 → Extras → Replacement Object tells you whether a table is a hybrid table and which object you should really be reading from. That single check can save a lot of confusing "the value is there in SE16 but missing in my view" situations.

entry.title: SAP Analytics Cloud: від таблиць Excel до розумних дашбордів
entry.content:
Скільки годин на тиждень ваші колеги витрачають на зведення даних із різних систем в Excel, щоб потім зробити звіт для керівництва? Якщо відповідь «занадто багато» - самий час познайомитися з SAP Analytics Cloud.

Що таке SAP Analytics Cloud?

SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) - це хмарна платформа для бізнес-аналітики, планування та прогнозування від SAP. Вона об'єднує три функції, які раніше потребували окремих інструментів: BI-звітність, корпоративне планування та предиктивна аналітика - все в одному браузерному застосунку.

Три стовпи SAP Analytics Cloud

• Business Intelligence - інтерактивні дашборди, аналіз у режимі реального часу, Stories (розповіді на основі даних)

• Planning - бюджетування, прогнозування, сценарне планування. Замінює громіздкі Excel-моделі

• Predictive Analytics - вбудовані ML-моделі для прогнозів без участі data scientist

Підключення до SAP-систем

SAC природно інтегрується з усіма SAP-джерелами: S/4HANA, BW/4HANA, Datasphere, SuccessFactors. Дані оновлюються в реальному часі або за розкладом. Це означає, що фінансовий директор бачить актуальний баланс не раз на місяць після закриття, а щомиті.

💡 Порівняння: Power BI і Tableau - потужні інструменти для загальної аналітики. SAC виграє там, де потрібна глибока інтеграція з SAP-даними, вбудоване планування та знання бізнес-семантики SAP. Якщо у вас SAP - SAC є природним вибором.

SAC для нетехнічних користувачів

Одна з ключових переваг SAC - доступність для бізнес-аналітиків без технічних знань. Drag-and-drop побудова дашбордів, автоматичні підказки системи («Чому зріс цей показник?»), Smart Insights та Smart Predict дозволяють отримувати аналітику без допомоги IT-відділу.

З чого почати?

1. SAP пропонує безкоштовний Trial-акаунт SAC на 90 днів - спробуйте підключити тестові дані

2. Почніть з побудови одного дашборду для реального бізнес-завдання вашої компанії

3. Вивчіть SAP Learning Journey «SAP Analytics Cloud» на офіційній платформі

Компанії, що переходять від Excel-звітності до SAC, зазвичай вражені не лише зручністю, а й тим, скільки помилок і розбіжностей ховалося в їхніх старих таблицях. Дані з однієї системи - надійніші за дані з десяти файлів.

entry.title: Understanding Production Version Consistency Checks in SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
Introduction to Production Versions
In SAP S/4HANA, a production version is a key element in the production planning process. It defines the specific combination of a Bill of Materials (BOM) and a routing that is used to produce a material. Each production version can have its own validity period and lot size range, allowing for flexibility in production planning.
 
Creating Production Versions
Production versions are created and managed in transaction C223. During creation, SAP performs consistency checks to ensure that the production version is valid and can be used in production processes.
 
What Are Consistency Checks?
Consistency checks are automated validations that ensure the integrity and usability of production versions. They verify that all necessary components, such as BOMs and routings, are correctly assigned and valid for use.
 
Customizing Consistency Checks
Why Is this Customizing Needed?
Production versions can become inconsistent over time due to changes in master data — for example, when a BOM is deleted, a routing validity expires, or a material is flagged for deletion. These inconsistencies can disrupt production planning and order creation.
 
SAP S/4HANA provides a mechanism to proactively detect and report these inconsistencies through consistency checks. However, not all inconsistencies are equally critical for every business. Some organizations may want to block production versions with missing BOMs (set as Error), while others may only want a warning to review later. This customizing allows you to configure which checks to perform and how strictly to enforce them.
 
How Consistency Checks Work
When consistency checks are performed on existing production versions — triggered either from the Process Production Version Fiori application or through Business Events — the system evaluates production versions based on the Issue Types configured in this customizing activity.
 
Any inconsistency of a particular Issue Type is reported based on the defined Issue Severity:
•Error: Blocks the production version from being used until the issue is resolved.
•Warning: Allows the production version to be used but flags it for review.
•Blank (Do Not Check): Skips the check entirely; the system will not evaluate this Issue Type.
 
Where to Configure
In SAP S/4HANA, you can customize how these consistency checks behave via the following path in the SAP IMG:
Production → Basic Data → Production Version → Define Inconsistency Checks for Production Versions
 
Configuration Activities
1. Change Issue Description and Issue Severity for any existing Issue Type, if required.
2. Choose Save.
 
Important Point to note before customizing
• Issue Severity can be set to Blank ('Do Not Check') if a specific Issue Type should not be evaluated.
•  Findings of any Issue Type can be reported as errors or warnings by setting the appropriate Issue Severity for that Issue Type.
•  F4 help does not work to select Issue Types; instead, you must directly enter the Issue Type codes (e.g., PVBOMMISS, PVTSLVALP) based on your requirements
 
Issue Types and Their Functionality
Below are some common issue types, their technical logic, and what they control:
 
Check CodeValidation MethodWhat This Check ControlsWhere System Looks (Table/Field)What System Checks ForSingle Check or Multiple ChecksPass ConditionFail ConditionRelated Master Data
PVMATREMIS_REM_INDICATOR_ACTIVEWhether material marked for deletion blocks production versionMARA-LVORM (Client level); MARC-LVORM (Plant level)If deletion flag is set at client or plant levelMultiple (checks both MARA and MARC)Both MARA-LVORM and MARC-LVORM are blank (not flagged for deletion)Either MARA-LVORM or MARC-LVORM is marked (X)Material Master (MM01/MM02)
PVMATSTATIS_MATERIAL_STATUS_INVALIDWhether material status allows production usageMARA-MSTAE (Cross-plant status); MARC-MMSTA (Plant-specific status)If status blocks production/MRP functionsMultiple (checks MARA first, then MARC takes precedence if maintained)Status is blank OR status allows production Status blocks production or MRPMaterial Master (MM01/MM02); Status config (OMT4)
PVBOMINACTIS_BOM_INACTIVEWhether BOM status allows usage in productionSTKO-STLST (BOM status); Reads via MAST (Material-to-BOM assignment)If BOM status = inactive or not released for productionSingle (checks only the BOM referenced in production version)BOM status = blank or status allows production usage (per OS23 config)BOM status is inactive or blocks production in OS23BOM Header (CS01/CS02); BOM status config (OS23)
PVBOMMISSIS_BOM_MISSINGWhether BOM referenced in production version actually existsMAST (Material-to-BOM assignment); STKO (BOM header)If combination of STLNR (BOM number) + STLAL (Alternative) exists and is validSingle (checks existence of specific BOM alternative)MAST entry exists for Material + Plant + BOM Alternative + Usage; STKO entry exists for STLNRNo MAST entry found OR STKO entry missing (BOM was deleted)BOM (CS01/CS02); Material Master BOM view
PVBOMVALPIS_BOM_TEMPORAL_INVALIDWhether BOM validity period overlaps with production version validitySTKO-DATUV (Valid-from date); Production version VERID (validity dates from MVER/MAPL)If BOM valid-from date ≤ production version valid-to AND BOM has no valid-to (open-ended) OR BOM valid-to ≥ production version valid-fromSingle (checks date overlap for one BOM)BOM validity period overlaps with production version validity periodNo date overlap between BOM and production version validity periodsBOM dates (CS02); Production version dates (C223)
PVBOMDELIS_BOM_TOBE_DELETEDWhether BOM is marked for deletionSTKO-LKENZ (Deletion indicator)If deletion flag is set in BOM headerSingle (checks deletion flag for referenced BOM)STKO-LKENZ is blank (not marked for deletion)STKO-LKENZ is marked (X = to be deleted)BOM Header (CS01/CS02)
PVBOMLOTSZIS_BOM_LOTSIZE_INVALIDWhether BOM lot size range is compatible with production versionSTKO-LOSBS (Lot size FROM); STKO-LOSVN (Lot size TO); Production version lot size fieldsIf production version lot size falls within BOM's defined rangeSingle (checks lot size range for one BOM)Production version lot size is within BOM's LOSBS to LOSVN range OR BOM has no lot size restriction (both blank)Production version lot size is outside BOM's defined rangeBOM Header lot size (CS02); Production version (C223)
PVTSLUSGIS_TASKLIST_USAGE_INVALIDWhether routing usage type is valid for productionPLKO-PLNTY (Task list type); PLKO-VERWE (Usage indicator); Reads via MAPL (Material-to-routing assignment)If routing usage = 1 (Production) or 3 (Universal) or other production-valid usageSingle (checks usage of routing referenced in production version)PLKO-VERWE = 1 (Production) OR 3 (Universal) as per T024W customizing Routing Header (CA01/CA02); Task list usage config (OP47)
PVTSLSTATIS_TASKLIST_STATUS_INVALIDWhether routing status allows production usagePLKO-STATU (Routing status)If routing status is released for productionSingle (checks status of routing referenced in production version)PLKO-STATU = 4 (Released) or blank OR status allows production  Routing Header (CA01/CA02);
PVTSLMISSIS_TASKLIST_MISSINGWhether routing referenced in production version actually existsMAPL (Material-to-routing assignment); PLKO (Routing header)If combination of PLNNR (Routing group) + PLNAL (Group counter) existsSingle (checks existence of specific routing)MAPL entry exists for Material + Plant + Routing group + Group counter; PLKO entry exists for PLNNR + PLNALNo MAPL entry found OR PLKO entry missing (routing was deleted)Routing (CA01/CA02); Material Master routing assignment
PVTSLVALPIS_TASKLIST_TEMPORAL_INVALIDWhether routing validity period overlaps with production version validityPLKO-DATUV (Valid-from date); PLKO-DATUB (Valid-to date); Production version validity datesIf routing valid-from ≤ production version valid-to AND routing valid-to ≥ production version valid-fromSingle (checks date overlap for one routing)Routing validity period overlaps with production version validity periodNo date overlap between routing and production version validity periodsRouting dates (CA02); Production version dates (C223)
PVTSLDELIS_TASKLIST_TOBE_DELETEDWhether routing is marked for deletionPLKO-LOEKZ (Deletion indicator)If deletion flag is set in routing headerSingle (checks deletion flag for referenced routing)PLKO-LOEKZ  is blank (not marked for deletion)PLKO-LOEKZ  is marked (X = to be deleted)Routing Header (CA01/CA02)
PVTSLPLNTIS_TASKLIST_PLANT_INVALIDWhether routing plant matches production version plantPLKO-WERKS (Routing plant)If routing plant = production version plantSingle (checks plant match for one routing)PLKO-WERKS matches production version plantPLKO-WERKS differs from production version plantRouting Header plant (CA01); Production version plant (C223)
PVTSLMATIS_MATERIAL_ASSIGNMENT_MISSINGWhether routing is assigned to the material in routing headerMAPL-MATNR (Material assignment in task list); PLKO (Routing header)If MAPL entry links material to routing groupSingle (checks MAPL entry existence for material-routing link)MAPL entry exists linking Material + Plant to Routing group (PLNNR) + Group counter (PLNAL)No MAPL entry exists (routing not assigned to material) even though PLKO existsRouting material assignment (CA01); MAPL table
PVTSLLOTSZIS_TASKLIST_LOTSIZE_INVALIDWhether routing lot size range is compatible with production versionPLKO-LOSVN (Lot size FROM); PLKO-LOSBS (Lot size TO); Production version lot sizeIf production version lot size falls within routing's defined rangeSingle (checks lot size range for one routing)Production version lot size is within routing's LOSVN to LOSBS range OR routing has no lot size restrictionProduction version lot size is outside routing's defined rangeRouting Header lot size (CA02); Production version (C223)
 
How to Use These Checks
 
When to use Error:
•Use when data integrity is critical, and any inconsistency must be resolved before proceeding
•Example: PVBOMMISS (missing BOM) should typically be an Error — you cannot produce without a BOM
 
When to use Warning:
•Use when you want to allow flexibility but still want to be alerted to potential issues
•Example: PVBOMVALP (BOM validity mismatch) might be a Warning if you have overlapping production versions
 
When to use Do Not Check (Blank):
•Use when the check is not relevant to your business process or during initial data setup
•Example: PVBOMLOTSZ (BOM lot size) might not be relevant if you don't use lot-size-specific BOMs
 
Understanding and configuring production version consistency checks is crucial for maintaining data integrity and ensuring smooth production planning in SAP S/4HANA. By customizing these checks, you can align the system's behavior with your business needs, ensuring that production versions are both valid and reliable.
 
#SAP
#SAPS4HANA
#SAPProductionPlanning
#SAPPP
#ProductionVersion

entry.title: Re: ABAP Cloud dereferencing REF TO DATA into DATA is not working for not released data types
entry.content: This behavior is intentional. When a data object is explicitly passed to an API in another software component, the data object’s type does not generally need to be released for the API to inspect and access the object. Data references define what can be addressed directly: if a reference is passed, the API can access and inspect the reference itself, but not the data object it points to, if the type is not released. The same applies to references contained within a passed data object, such as a structure component that holds a reference. This approach supports generic frameworks by avoiding the need to generally release every type when an object of that type is explicitly passed to an API in another software component.

entry.title: TRM - Intercompany postings in second ledger with CME
entry.content:
I've tried to post an intercompany loan in TRM, using the "Process Business Transactions" app, but I received the following error: Special G/L indicator not defined or incorrect - screenshot attached.

But the principal ledger posted through the TBB1 is correct, and the configuration is the same.

Interest Flow 9209 - MZZ12+ MZZ12-

 

 

 

 

 

 

We have try to solve it though:

 

  1. Differentiate Posting Specs:
    • If using mirror transactions or complex intercompany flows, create a dedicated Posting Spec for "Nopaying Valuation Areas" instead of sharing one with "Paying Valuation Areas." – Even this is the recommended option is not working.
    • Ensure the Special G/L Indicator field remains consistent across both versions for the same update type.
  2. Verify Account Symbol Assignment:
    • Confirm that Account Symbol V.0 is only associated with Account Type K (Vendors).
    • Confirm that Account Symbol C.0 is only associated with Account Type D (Customers).
  3. Check Special G/L Indicator Mapping:
    • Ensure the Special G/L indicator used in the TRM posting spec (e.g. indicator "E") is valid for the account type assigned to the posting key (e.g. key "19" for Customers).

entry.title: Re: Problem: @ui5/cli@4.0.57 has a dependency on pacote@19.0.2 which has a high vulnerability
entry.content:
Hi smarchesini,

hier is a copy from package.json:

{
  "name": "xxxxxxxxxx",
  "version": "0.0.1",
  "description": "xxxxxxxx",
  "keywords": [
    "ui5",
    "openui5",
    "sapui5"
  ],
  "main": "webapp/index.html",
  "dependencies": {},
  "devDependencies": {
    "@ui5/cli": "^4.0.33",
    "@sap/ux-ui5-tooling": "1",
    "@sap-ux/eslint-plugin-fiori-tools": "^10.0.0",
    "eslint": "^10",
    "@sap-ux/ui5-middleware-fe-mockserver": "2",
    "rimraf": "^5.0.5"
  },
  "overrides": {
    "vulnerable-sub-package-name": "^secure.version.number"
  },
  "scripts": {
    "start": "fiori run --open \"test/flp.html#app-preview\"",
    "start-local": "fiori run --config ./ui5-local.yaml --open \"test/flp.html#app-preview\"",
    "build": "ui5 build --config=ui5.yaml --clean-dest --dest dist",
    "lint": "eslint ./",
    "start-mock": "fiori run --config ./ui5-mock.yaml --open \"test/flp.html#app-preview\"",
    "deploy": "npm run build && fiori deploy --config ui5-deploy.yaml",
    "deploy-config": "fiori add deploy-config",
    "start-noflp": "fiori run --open \"/index.html?sap-ui-xx-viewCache=false\"",
    "start-variants-management": "fiori run --open \"/preview.html#app-preview\"",
    "undeploy": "npm run build && fiori undeploy --config ui5-deploy.yaml",
    "deploy-test": "npm run build && fiori deploy --config ui5-deploy.yaml --testMode true"
  },
  "sapuxLayer": "CUSTOMER_BASE",
  "sapux": true
}

entry.title: Re: Supported Ways to Transfer AL11 Files from S/4HANA RI...
entry.content:
Hi @vidyasagar1 ,

There is not a direct migration for this use case. You can evaluate the following options:

- Install Edge Integration Cell and use NFS adapter (Complex architecture, requires lots of effort).

- Expose the AL11 path with SFTP (in Linux is very simple) and use SFTP adapter (My preferred option). You can use TCP tunneling in Cloud Connector to access the SFTP path from CPI. 

- Use a SMB server and use the "new" SMB adapter https://help.sap.com/docs/integration-suite/isuite-integrations-and-apis/smb-adapter (I don't know what is the effort needed to configure a SMB server, I have never tried it).

Hope it helps.

Regards,

Roberto

entry.title: How to Create a Business Service in SAP Cloud ALM Integration Monitoring
entry.content:
Introduction

Integration Monitoring in SAP Cloud ALM gives operations teams a centralised view of message flows across hybrid landscapes. A key building block of that view is the Business Service — a logical grouping that maps technical services or systems to a recognisable business context/transaction. This post walks through exactly how to create one.

 

Prerequisites

Before you start, make sure the following are in place:

  • The Integration Architect role has been assigned in SAP Cloud ALM.
  • You have configured the required service or systems in Integration Monitoring

 

Step 1: Open Integration Monitoring Configuration

Log in to SAP Cloud ALM and navigate to the Operations section. Select Integration & Exception Monitoring, then choose the Configuration tab and click on the Business Services section

 

Step 2: Create a New Business Service

Click the Add button in the Business Services panel. A creation dialog opens. Provide the following:

  • Name: A short, descriptive identifier (e.g. "BPReplicationFromS4HANAtoSalesServiceCloud").
  • Description: Optional but recommended — explain what business process this service covers

 

Step 3: Assign Services or Systems

Once the Business Service is saved, open it and navigate to the details page. Click Add in the Services/Systems section and select from the list of available services/systems available in your landscape.

 

Step 4: Configure Filtering 

To ensure only relevant messages are displayed in the monitoring UI, use the Filter for Data section to scope the Business Service to a specific business context.

To add a filter:

  • Click Add in the Filter for Data section.
  • Define a name for the filter and the required filter attributes.
  • Once the filter is created, default event configurations are created automatically.

 

 

Step 5: Define the Path of the Business Service

The Paths tab in the Business Service configuration allows you to define the direction of message flow between the services and systems assigned to the Business Service. This is a visual configuration — it controls how the message flow is rendered in the monitoring UI, not which messages are monitored.

To configure paths:

  • Open the Business Service and navigate to the Paths tab.
  • A matrix is displayed with all assigned services and systems listed as both rows (source) and columns (target).
  • Select the checkbox at the intersection of a source and target to define the direction of that message flow.
  • Click Save to apply your configuration.

Once configured, the monitoring UI renders a flow diagram showing the direction of messages across your landscape. For example, selecting S4D.100 → MyCPI_01 → MySalesandServiceCloudV2_01 produces a visual flow from SAP S/4HANA through SAP Integration Suite (Cloud Integration) to SAP Sales Cloud and SAP Service Cloud Version 2, as shown below.

 

Step 6: Verify in the Monitoring Dashboard

Return to the Integration Monitoring overview. Your newly created Business Service should now be available in the scope selector. Select the Business Service to view the monitoring data

 

More Information

For a full reference on Integration Monitoring configuration, monitoring and eventing options, refer to the official SAP Help Portal documentation:

https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-alm/applicationhelp/integration-monitoring

 

Closing Thoughts

Business Services are a small configuration effort with a large payoff. Once in place, they give your operations team a business-centric view of integration health.
