entry.title: Consolidation of Multiple Purchase Requisitions into a Single RFQ Item in S/4 HANA or in SAP Ariba
entry.content:
Hi Experts

I have a scenario where we receive multiple Purchase Requisitions (PRs) for the same material from different plants across various city offices.

Example:

Material: ABC123

  • PR 100000001 – Plant A – Quantity: 100
  • PR 100000002 – Plant B – Quantity: 150
  • PR 100000003 – Plant C – Quantity: 200

Business Requirement:

The business wants to create a single RFQ line item for the material with the consolidated quantity (450 in the above example), instead of creating separate RFQ line items for each PR/plant combination. At the same time, the individual PR references should remain traceable for follow-on processing.

I understand that this is an SAP limitation, as consolidation is only possible at the plant level. However, if we create multiple RFQ line items for the same material across different plants, duplicate line items will be sent to Ariba for the vendor. This is not logical, as the vendor would be expected to quote the same price for the same material across multiple line items.

Has anyone encountered this scenario before? Is there any workaround or solution that would allow us to meet this business requirement?

Thanks.

entry.title: **Title:** [S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition] Is there an API to post an intracompany asset transfer (As
entry.content:
**Title:** [S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition] Is there an API to post an intracompany asset transfer (Asset-to-Asset / ABUMN equivalent)?

**Body:**

I'm working on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (2602) and want to perform an intracompany asset transfer (asset A → asset B within the same company code, i.e. the equivalent of transaction ABUMN) from an external system via API.

Here's what I've found so far:

- **Fixed Asset - Master Data** (OData V4, `api_fixedasset`) — master data CRUD only, no transfer transaction.
- **Journal Entry - Post** (SOAP, communication scenario SAP_COM_0002 / `JournalEntryBulkCreateRequest`) — can post journal entries, but it's unclear how to reproduce the transfer-specific logic (proportional net book value transfer across all depreciation areas).

My questions:

1. Is there a **standard released API** (OData or SOAP) to post an intracompany asset transfer (ABUMN equivalent)?
2. If so, **which API / communication scenario** should I use?
3. If possible, could you share a **sample payload** for a transfer such as:
- Transfer within the same company code
- Sending asset: `6100000-1`
- Receiving asset: `83000000-1`

If no dedicated API exists, I'd also appreciate guidance on the recommended approach for replicating this via the Journal Entry API (e.g. which asset transaction type to use).

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: SAP EXAM - TRAINING AND CERTIFICATION
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I’m new to the SAP ecosystem and I’m currently transitioning from the publishing world into SAP S/4HANA Finance.

Last Thursday I purchased my certification exam through the SAP Store. I received the initial email saying that my order was being processed, but I never received the confirmation that the order was completed.

I contacted the SAP Store the next day and they told me this was due to technical issues on their side. Earlier this week I followed up again, and they replied that they are still working on it, but without giving any indication of when it might be resolved.

As someone who is new to SAP, I find this situation a bit surprising coming from a global technology leader, and I’m not sure what the usual procedure is in cases like this.

Has anyone experienced something similar? Is there a specific support channel for technical issues related to certification exam access or order completion?

Any guidance would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: Re: 🐍 July Developer Challenge Week 1 — Model the Board
entry.content:
SAP Developer Challenge - Week 1 Submission

Here is my submission for the Week 1 Developer Challenge (Snake & Ladder Game).

I created a CAP Node.js project named snake-ladder and modeled the board squares, game sessions, players, and turn status flow.

db/schema.cds

```cds
namespace snakeladder;

type TurnStatus : String enum {
Waiting;
Playing;
Moving;
Blocked;
Finished;
};

entity BoardSquares {
key square : Integer;
snakeTo : Integer;
ladderTo : Integer;
isDoubleHead: Boolean default false;
}

entity GameSessions {
key ID : UUID;
name : String(100);
createdAt : Timestamp;
startedAt : Timestamp;
finishedAt : Timestamp;
winner : Association to Players;
}

@flow.status: TurnStatus
entity Players {
key ID : UUID;
session : Association to GameSessions;
name : String(100);
@readonly
position : Integer default 1;
@readonly
TurnStatus : TurnStatus default #Waiting;
turnOrder : Integer;
lastRoll : Integer;
}
```

srv/game-service.cds

```cds
using { snakeladder } from '../db/schema';

@Path: 'GameService'
service GameService {

entity GameSessions as projection on snakeladder.GameSessions;

@flow.status: TurnStatus
entity Players as projection on snakeladder.Players actions {

// Week 2 will implement these - declare them now so the model is complete.
@from: [ #Waiting ]
@TO: #Playing
action startTurn();

@from: [ #Playing ]
@TO: #Moving
action rollDice() returns Integer;

@from: [ #Moving ]
@TO: #Waiting
action confirmMove();

@from: [ #Moving ]
@TO: #Blocked
action blockPlayer();

@from: [ #Blocked ]
@TO: $flow.previous
action unblockPlayer();

@from: [ #Moving ]
@TO: #Finished
action winGame();
}

entity BoardSquares as projection on snakeladder.BoardSquares;
}
```

Screenshot: https://files.catbox.moe/7vcv93.png

The screenshot shows http://localhost:4004 with the GameService endpoints: GameSessions, Players, and BoardSquares.

For TurnStatus, I kept the proposed enum values (Waiting, Playing, Moving, Blocked, and Finished) because they clearly cover the full player lifecycle: waiting for a turn, rolling the dice, moving on the board, being blocked by a special square, and finishing the game.

entry.title: Max Innovation Workshop 'Agentic AI Day: Empowering SAP Customers with Agentic AI' (July 15, 2026)
entry.content:
Virtual

July 15, 2026

Agenda

Dear Valued Customer,

We are excited to invite you to the Max Innovation Workshop on the topic ‘Agentic AI Day: Empowering SAP Customers with Agentic AI’ which will be held virtually on July 15, 2026. You can find the detailed agenda for the workshop here.

Join us for a high-impact day where SAP leaders, industry experts, and SAP MaxAttention or Max Success Plan customers come together to explore the future of Agentic AI and the Autonomous Enterprise. This exclusive event is crafted to help you accelerate your transformation journey with practical insights, live demonstrations, and direct access to SAP’s architects and experts.

What to expect:

  • Keynote & Anchor Demo – An end-to-end agentic AI workflow solving a real business challenge
  • Customer Roundtable – Candid transformation stories from enterprise leaders at various customers
  • SAP’s Autonomous Enterprise Showcase – Deep dives into Autonomous Spend, Finance, SCM, and Customer Experience
  • SAP Business Data Cloud – How SAP Business Data Cloud powers AI Agents with unified enterprise knowledge
  • Hands-On Sessions – Activate a standard SAP Agent or build your own using Joule Studio 2.0
  • Wrap-Up & Max Success Plan – Learn how to start or scale your Autonomous Enterprise journey

At the end of the workshop, attendees will leave with a clear understanding of how to design, deploy, govern, and measure AI agents, along with actionable next steps.

Our Workshops

These sessions and workshop series focus on a specific topic and the purpose is to enable our customers to understand the product portfolio and innovative technologies of SAP in order to drive adoption and consumption. As specifically now, it is more important than ever to help companies to be successful and to respond to changing markets by transforming into intelligent enterprises in the experience economy.

Important

Please note that the 'Attend' button which can be found within this blog post does not serve as a formal registration for the event.

To officially register, please reach out to me, paul.schropp@sap.com and I will ensure that your registration is properly processed.

We look forward to having you join us for our upcoming events and thank you for your understanding!

Your feedback

Feel free to share your feedback and thoughts in the comment section below.

entry.title: Unable to auto create POs from source assigned PRs in case of stock transport order bet two Sloc
entry.content:
Hi Team,
We are having a scenario where we carryout stock transport order between two storage locations using the document type UD. The process begins with running a MRP and generating STO PR's on the supplying storage location. Now, these PR's are to be converted to PO using "schedule purchasing job" (automatic conversion of PR to PO). Since the PR's are assigned with both supplying storage location and plant, we expect this to be converted to PO automatically. However, this is not happening. When we maintain source list with same plant as supplying plant, system does create auto PO. However, for some of the products when we are trying to maintain the source list, it gives an error "Active Source List exists for Material: NAU98113G Plant: GP01 Combination.". This is because we already have maintained the sourcelist for external suppliers for our regular purchases (Please note, this is NOT a fixed source of supply). 
So, can you kindly let us know how can we maintain the sourcelist for both external suppliers and supplying plant (for STO's). If this is not possible, then why would system not convert the PR to PO automatically even when required source exists on the PR.

Thanks.

entry.title: Enabling IAS for SAP SuccessFactors RCM and RMK(Internal Career Site)
entry.content:
Introduction

In this blog post, I will walk through the process of enabling Identity Authentication Service (IAS) for SAP SuccessFactors Recruiting Management (RCM) and Recruiting Marketing (RMK) for an Internal Career Site. While the configuration steps are straightforward, ensuring that each component is correctly aligned is critical, as small inconsistencies can lead to issues such as login loops, failed redirects, or incomplete authentication.

This guide covers the required prerequisites, configuration steps, and troubleshooting scenarios based on practical observations to help you implement a secure and seamless authentication experience.

 

Prerequisites

Before enabling IAS for the Internal Career Site, ensure the following prerequisites are completed.

1. Identity Authentication Service (IAS)

IAS must be enabled for your SAP SuccessFactors instance. IAS acts as the Identity Provider and handles user authentication. Without IAS, SAML-based login for the Internal Career Site cannot be configured.

2. Career Site Builder Setup

Career Site Builder (CSB) must be configured and integrated with Recruiting Management (RCM). This integration enables seamless navigation and authentication between the career site and recruiting modules.

3. Recruiter Single Sign-On (SSO)

Recruiter SSO should be enabled to provide a consistent authentication experience across Recruiting Management and Recruiting Marketing.

If Recruiter SSO is not maintained, recruiters will not experience true single sign-on. Even after successfully logging in, they will be prompted to enter their username and password again when navigating between interconnected modules such as RCM and RMK. This breaks the seamless user experience and can create confusion, especially during testing, as it may appear that IAS is not functioning correctly.

4. Career Site and Recruiting Management Integration

Career Site–RCM redirects must be enabled.

Navigation:
Provisioning → Configure Recruiting Marketing Datacenter URL

Maintain the Recruiting Marketing datacenter URL that corresponds to your tenant region. 

If the datacenter URL is incorrect, users may experience failed redirects when accessing the internal career site. In practice, this can appear as the page not loading correctly or users being redirected to an invalid or blank page. In some cases, authentication seems to initiate but does not complete, which can be misleading since the issue is related to incorrect endpoint configuration rather than IAS itself.

5. Career Site Builder Integration Settings

Navigation:
Admin Center → Manage Career Site Builder → Site Configuration → Site Integration

Verify the following configurations in Career Site Builder Integration Settings:

  • Company ID
    Copy the Company ID from Provisioning. In training instances, ensure it is maintained in ALL CAPS.

  • Deployment Data Center
    Select the correct data center where your SAP SuccessFactors HCM instance is hosted.

  • Single Sign-On Security Key
    Maintain the security key (20–28 characters). This key acts as a bridge between RCM and RMK and is required for secure communication.

  • Recruiter Sync (Single Sign-On)
    Enable Recruiter Sync to allow synchronization of recruiter information and provide access to Career Site Builder and Recruiting Advanced Analytics.

In practice, when any of these configurations are incorrect or inconsistent, the integration between RCM and RMK does not fail immediately but leads to issues during usage. Recruiter Sync may not function properly, recruiters may be unable to access Career Site Builder, and job or candidate data may not synchronize as expected. These issues can be misleading, as the initial configuration appears complete, but the breakdown in connectivity between systems becomes visible only when recruiters start working with the system.

6. Mobile Apply and Internal Career Site

Ensure both Mobile Apply and Internal Career Site are enabled.

Navigation:
Admin Center → Manage Recruiting Sites

These settings allow employees to access and apply for internal jobs. If the Internal Career Site is not enabled, users will not be able to access internal opportunities even if IAS is configured successfully.


Step 1: Configure SAP SuccessFactors (RCM) for IAS

The first step is to establish trust between SAP SuccessFactors and Identity Authentication Service (IAS). This allows SuccessFactors to redirect users to IAS for authentication and accept the SAML assertion returned after a successful login.

Navigation

Go to:

Admin Center → Manage Service Provider Configuration for Identity Authentication Service

Enable SAML Integration

Under the Import IdP SAML Metadata section:

  1. Turn on SAML Integration
  2. Maintain the IAS Tenant Name

Note:
The tenant name is derived from the IAS URL.

Example:

https://a122u123.accounts.cloud.sap

Tenant Name: a122u123

An issue we encountered during this step was maintaining an incorrect tenant name. Although the configuration appeared to be correct, it resulted in an endless login loop where users were redirected to IAS, entered their credentials, and were sent back to the login page again instead of being authenticated. This made it difficult to identify the root cause initially, as the issue appeared to be related to authentication rather than a simple tenant mismatch.

Export Metadata

After completing the configuration:

  1. Click Save
  2. Click Export Metadata

Expected outcome: The metadata file is successfully generated and available for download. This file will be imported into IAS when creating the RCM application.

Step 2: Configure IAS Application for Recruiting Management (RCM)

Next, create an application in IAS to establish the trust relationship with your SAP SuccessFactors Recruiting Management instance. This application will use the metadata exported in the previous step to enable SAML-based authentication.

Navigation

Go to:

Applications & Resources → Applications

Create New Application

Click Create and maintain the following details as shown in the configuration screen:

  • Display Name: RCM_Tenant_name
  • Home URL: Leave blank (or maintain if required by your landscape)
  • Type: SAP SuccessFactors solution
  • Parent Application: None
  • Organization ID: global
  • Protocol Type: SAML 2.0

After maintaining the above details, click Create.

Configure SAML 2.0 Settings

  1. Open the newly created application
  2. Navigate to SAML 2.0 Configuration
  3. Upload the metadata file that was exported from SuccessFactors (RCM system)

Configure Certificate Settings

  1. Go to the Certificates section
  2. Maintain:
    • Signing Algorithm: SHA-256

SHA-256 is the recommended signing algorithm and helps ensure secure SAML communication between IAS and SuccessFactors.

Maintain Subject Name Identifier

This is one of the most important settings in the configuration, as it determines how IAS identifies users when sending the SAML assertion to SuccessFactors.

Maintain the following values:

  • Source: Identity Directory
  • Attribute: Login Name

The attribute specifies which user field from IAS will be passed to SuccessFactors as the user's identifier. In our case, we used Login Name as the attribute, but this can vary depending on your organization’s configuration. It is important to ensure that the selected attribute aligns with the user identifier maintained in SuccessFactors, otherwise users may authenticate in IAS but not be able to access the system.

Summary

At this stage, IAS has been enabled for SAP SuccessFactors Recruiting Management (RCM), and the required SAML configuration has been completed. The system is now prepared for the next phase of the Internal Career Site authentication setup.

Step 3: Configure Career Site Builder (RMK) for IAS

After enabling IAS for Recruiting Management (RCM), the next step is to establish trust between Recruiting Marketing (RMK) / Career Site Builder (CSB) and IAS. This allows employees accessing the Internal Career Site to be redirected to IAS for authentication instead of using the default login mechanism.

Navigation

Log in to your SuccessFactors instance and go to:

Admin Center → Manage Career Site Builder

Then navigate to:

Settings → IDP Configuration

Maintain IAS Tenant Details

In the Import IDP SAML Metadata section:

  1. Enter the IAS Tenant Name
  2. Click Save

The tenant name identifies which IAS tenant RMK should use for authentication. If the tenant name is incorrect, users may be redirected to the wrong authentication endpoint or encounter SAML-related errors when attempting to access the Internal Career Site.

Note:
Make sure you save the configuration before proceeding to metadata generation.

Generate and Export Metadata

After saving:

  1. Click Generate
  2. Click Export Metadata

Expected outcome: RMK metadata is generated successfully and can be imported into the RMK application in IAS.

The exported metadata file contains the RMK Service Provider information that IAS requires to establish trust. Think of this metadata as the configuration blueprint that allows IAS and RMK to recognize and trust each other during the authentication process.

Step 4: Create IAS Application for RMK

With the RMK metadata available, the next step is to create a dedicated application in IAS. Although Recruiting Management (RCM) and Recruiting Marketing (RMK) are closely connected, each application requires its own trust configuration in IAS.

Log in to the SAP Cloud Identity Services (IAS) Admin Console.

Navigation

Go to:

Applications & Resources → Applications

Create New Application

Click Create and maintain the following details (as shown in your configuration screen):

  • Display Name: RMK_Tenant_name
  • Home URL: Leave blank (or maintain if required)
  • Type: SAP SuccessFactors solution
  • Parent Application: None
  • Organization ID: global
  • Protocol Type: SAML 2.0

Click Create.

Configure SAML 2.0 Settings

  1. Open the newly created RMK application
  2. Navigate to SAML 2.0 Configuration
  3. Upload the metadata file exported from Career Site Builder

This step completes the trust configuration from the IAS side and allows IAS to recognize authentication requests originating from RMK.

Configure Signing Settings

  1. Go to Certificates
  2. Verify:
    • Signing Algorithm: SHA-256

This ensures that SAML assertions exchanged between IAS and RMK are signed using a secure and supported algorithm.

Maintain Subject Name Identifier

Ensure consistency with the RCM configuration:

  • Subject Name Identifier
    • Source: Identity Directory
    • Attribute: loginName

Important Notes

  • Always save changes before generating metadata in Career Site Builder
  • Separate applications must be created in IAS for:
    • RCM
    • RMK
  • Ensure the same Subject Name Identifier configuration is maintained across all applications
  • Ensure that the IAS application is created as a standalone application and not as a child application

Troubleshooting

Issue 1: Internal candidate unable to apply and repeatedly redirected to sign-in page

One issue I encountered during testing was an endless redirect loop when attempting to access the Internal Career Site. Authentication appeared to start correctly, but users were continuously returned to the sign-in page.

After investigation, the root cause was a tenant domain mismatch. The system was configured with the legacy domain format:

<tenant_name>.accounts.ondemand.com

However, the instance had already been migrated to the Common Super Domain (CSD) framework and was using:

<tenant_name>.accounts.cloud.sap

  • Ensure that this updated tenant name is maintained consistently in:

    • RCM (Manage Service Provider Configuration)
    • Career Site Builder → IDP Configuration

  • Save the changes and retry the access.

Issue 2: User successfully authenticates in IAS but cannot access the Internal Career Site

If users can log in through IAS but are not successfully signed in to SuccessFactors, verify the Subject Name Identifier configuration.

The attribute selected in IAS must correspond to the user identifier maintained in SuccessFactors. If the values do not match, IAS can authenticate the user successfully, but SuccessFactors will be unable to identify the corresponding account.

Review the Subject Name Identifier configuration in both the RCM and RMK IAS applications and confirm that the selected attribute aligns with your organization's user mapping strategy.

Conclusion

Enabling IAS for SAP SuccessFactors Recruiting Management (RCM) and Recruiting Marketing (RMK) requires configuration across multiple components, including SuccessFactors, Career Site Builder, and IAS. While the setup itself is straightforward, attention to details such as tenant configuration, metadata exchange, and user identifier mapping is critical for a successful implementation.

By following the steps outlined in this guide, you can establish the trust relationships required for SAML-based authentication and provide a seamless sign-on experience for internal career site users. If issues arise, validating tenant settings and authentication mappings early in the troubleshooting process can help resolve most common configuration problems.

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA OTC – Automating Minimum Order Surcharge Using AMIW/AMIZ + MV45AFZZ Enhancement
entry.content:
Hi Robert,

Thank you for reviewing this requirement.

The condition was initially configured through the access sequence; however, we are still evaluating how customer-specific exemptions should be handled before automatically triggering the surcharge condition.

Regarding the use of pricing routines, we explored that option, but it did not fully satisfy the business requirements. As a result, we implemented an enhancement in MV45AFZZ during the sales order save process. The enhancement performs the following validations:

  • Checks the minimum order value threshold.
  • Verifies whether the customer is exempt from the surcharge.
  • Proposes or updates the surcharge condition value accordingly.

In addition, the surcharge determination process is not globally standardized across all countries. Certain regions have unique business requirements and exemption criteria, which require country-specific logic within the enhancement. This level of flexibility could not be effectively achieved through standard pricing routines alone.

The blog focuses on a relatively simple and straightforward business requirement, which may raise the question of why an enhancement in MV45AFZZ was chosen instead of a pricing routine. The primary reason is the need to accommodate customer exemptions and regional variations in the surcharge determination logic while maintaining flexibility for future business requirements.

I hope this provides additional context behind the design decision.

entry.title: Re: To check feasibility of adding column in COOIS report...
entry.content:
Dear @subraya_joshi87ec66,

Adding a calculated column directly in standard Monitor Production / Planned Orders (COOIS) -> Components is not supported by standard Fiori personalization or Adapt UI. The technical app information shows Framework ID = GUI not FIORI. Therefore the standard layout can only be used to display fields that are already available in the output. SAP KBA 3363122 confirms that available custom fields can be displayed via Change Layout but this does not create a new calculated column

The recommended solution is to create a separate custom report using Custom CDS Views / Custom Analytical Queries. Based on the available manufacturing order component data source, for example I_MfgOrderOperationComponent a calculated field can be added, for example like I do below:

Missing Qty = RequiredQuantity – WithdrawnQuantity

IMPORTANT: In the calculation editor the unit reference must be removed, otherwise the view will not activate (message SD_CDS_ENTITY131). Use GET_NUMERIC_VALUE(RequiredQuantity) - GET_NUMERIC_VALUE(WithdrawnQuantity) and add Base Unit of Measure as a separate column for context

 

This would provide a new Fiori analytical report/tile with the required column but it would not change the standard COOIS screen itself. SAP Help confirms that Custom CDS Views support calculations and that Custom Analytical Queries can be used for reporting and analysis. As a temporary workaround the COOIS Components list can be exported to Excel and the difference can be calculated there

If the requirement is specifically to add this field directly into standard COOIS option is raised to SAP via SAP Customer Influence

More details below:

SAP Help – Monitor Production / Planned Orders (COOIS)
SAP KBA 3363122 – Custom field is not displayed in app “Monitor Production / Planned Orders”
SAP Help – Creating Custom CDS Views
SAP Help – Adding Calculations in Custom CDS Views
SAP Help – Custom Analytical Queries and Services
SAP KBA 2963059 – How to Submit Feature Requests for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Calculation View Features of 2026 QRC2
entry.content:
Within the time frame of 2026 Q2, several new calculation view features have been released in SAP Business Application Studio when connected to SAP HANA Cloud database QRC2. Some of these features are highlighted below. You can find examples that illustrate the individual features here. An overview of features of other releases can be found here.

Optimized Redeployment

When a calculation view is redeployed in a stacked scenario, all dependent objects in the same HDI container are normally dropped and sequentially recreated. This default behavior avoids cascading revalidations but comes with a cost: privileges on dependent objects are temporarily, or in cross-container scenarios potentially permanently, revoked, and the full recreate cycle adds significant deployment time.

Optimized redeployment addresses this. Instead of dropping all dependents upfront, a consistency check determines which dependent views are still valid after the change. Views unaffected by the redeployed calculation view are kept as-is and skipped entirely. This reduces both the time spent recreating unaffected views and the privilege reassignment overhead.

To enable optimized redeployment for calculation views, add the following parameter to the hdi-deploy invocation in package.json:

"start": "node node_modules/@sap/hdi-deploy/deploy.js --parameter com.sap.hana.di.calculationview/optimized_redeploy=true"

A global parameter (optimized_redeploy=true) is also available and activates the behavior for all HDI object types that support it.

Replace Instead of Drop and Recreate During Redeployment

By default, calculation views are dropped and recreated during redeployment. Dropping a view revokes all privileges associated with it. Privileges defined in HDI roles of the same container are automatically regranted at the end of deployment, but direct privileges held by roles outside the current HDI container are permanently lost.

The new replace option substitutes the drop-and-recreate cycle with an in-place replacement. Because the object is never dropped, existing privileges are preserved throughout deployment. The benefit is most significant when combined with optimized redeployment: without it, dependent views are dropped before the root view is replaced, so they never benefit from the replace logic. With both options active, the replace strategy also applies to dependent views.

To enable the replace behavior, pass the following parameter:

"start": "node node_modules/@sap/hdi-deploy/deploy.js --parameter optimized_replace=true"

Combining optimized_replace=true with optimized_redeploy=true gives the strongest deployment time and privilege preservation benefit.

 

Active/Active Read-Enabled Hints

In an Active/Active (Read-Enabled) setup, query routing hints can now be configured at the individual calculation view level. This gives fine-grained control over whether a query is directed to the primary system or the read replica, allowing workload to be distributed and overall throughput to be increased.

 

Hints are evaluated only on calculation views that are directly referenced in the query. 

 

Switching from a Join Node to a Non-Equi Join Node

A Join node can now be converted directly to a Non-Equi Join node via the node's context menu, without needing to manually rebuild the join logic.

During conversion you can choose how the resulting join condition is expressed: either graphically, based on the column display,

or as an explicit join expression.

This simplifies the refactoring of join logic and reduces manual rework. Note that features not supported by Non-Equi Join nodes, such as dynamic joins, text joins, and filter expressions, are removed automatically during the conversion.

Calculation View Status Dialog

When opening a calculation view with certain properties, a status dialog now appears and reports details about the current view state. Where applicable, the dialog also offers direct actions to resolve issues, for example, prompting you to log in to Cloud Foundry when no active database connection is found.

The dialog covers four categories:

  • Read-only mode: reasons why the calculation view was opened in read-only mode
  • Database connection: current connection status to the database
  • Data classification inconsistencies: detected mismatches in the data classification setting
  • BDC integration: whether the calculation view is used with BDC integration mode

 

Calculated Measures in MDS Cubes

Calculated measures can now be added to MDS Cubes and are evaluated at query runtime. This makes it possible to execute calculations at query runtime.

If a calculated measure cannot be translated into a valid MDS formula, deployment fails with a corresponding error message.

entry.title: What's New in SAP Subscription Billing (Q2 2026)
entry.content:
The past quarter saw the delivery of several technical enhancements and feature refinements, while major features were in development for release in the second half of the year. Let's look at a couple of notable features from Q2:   

Faster Creation of Price Conditions - Copy and Adjust 

You can now copy an existing price condition and modify the data to create a new condition. This enables you to define similar conditions much faster in the Manage Lookup Table Data app. 

End‑of‑Month Billing Cycle - Improved Alignment

For anniversary-billed subscriptions that start at the end of a month (29th, 30th, or 31st day), the billing cycle reference date is no longer shifted to the first day of the current or next month. The original subscription start date is retained as the billing cycle reference date, even if the end-of-month rule is Bill from Current Cycle.  This aligns seamlessly with anniversary billing cycles and proration settings.

Reference dates on the 29th, 30th, or 31st day of the month are now also supported by the Bill from Current Cycle rule.

Lastly, billing cycle dates are now intelligently adapted for months without the specified calendar day:

  • February: Set to the last day of February, so 28th or 29th in leap years.
  • Months with 30 days when the reference date is the 31st: Set to the 30th.

For more information, see Improved Behavior for End-of-Month Rule "Bill from Current Cycle". Note that if your tenant was created before June 8, 2026, you'll need to activate the feature in the Manage Business Configuration app. As with all features that can be activated by customers, the feature will be automatically activated by SAP in the future, in this case on October 8, 2026.

We'll be back at the end of Q3 with an overview of the latest features. Until then, keep innovating!

___________________________________________________________

Subscribe to Never Miss New Features

To get notified about future update blogs, subscribe to SAP Subscription Billing in the SAP Community:

We also encourage you to subscribe to our What's New page on SAP Help Portal for a full list of updates:

More Information About SAP Subscription Billing:
  • SAP Help Portal Page
  • Feature Overview

entry.title: Re: Subcontract PO Qty With BOM component Qty excess Qty...
entry.content:
In subcontracting, SAP does not automatically enforce a strict 1:1 relationship between 101 and 543. The 543 movement is proposed based on the BOM quantity, but the user can manually change it in MIGO. Standard tolerance checks (OMCQ) are designed for over‑delivery/under‑delivery of PO items, not for subcontracting component consumption. That is why your OMCQ attempt didn’t work.

If you want to restrict this, you need an enhancement point (MB_DOCUMENT_BADI or EXIT_SAPMM07M_001) to enforce the control which should validate that the 543 movement quantity does not exceed the 101 movement quantity (or PO component qty), and issue an error message if violated.

entry.title: Multi-System Joule Support for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition
entry.content:
Introduction

In today's enterprise landscape, organizations rarely operate with just one SAP S/4HANA system. Whether driven by complex organizational structures, regional requirements, or company acquisitions, businesses typically maintain multiple S/4HANA Cloud systems across their landscape. Each system serves specific purposes: perhaps one handles finance for EMEA, another manages operations in North America, and yet another supports a recently acquired subsidiary.

Until recently, Joule faced a significant limitation: it could only connect to a single SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud system. This meant that users working across multiple systems couldn't leverage Joule's AI capabilities uniformly across their entire landscape. Organizations found themselves in a challenging position: either create multiple Joule instances (one for each S/4HANA system) or accept limited coverage of their system landscape.
The good news? Joule now supports multi-system connectivity for both SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition (SAP Help Documentation). With this enhancement, you can configure one Joule instance to connect to multiple S/4HANA systems, providing your users with a unified, intelligent assistant experience across their entire landscape.
 

What Multi-System Support Enables

With multi-system Joule, you can:
Unified Experience: One Joule instance serves all your S/4HANA Private Cloud systems
Intelligent System Selection: Joule automatically determines the right system based on user prompts and authorizations
Cross-Landscape Coverage: Users access scenarios across different systems in a single conversation
Simplified Administration: Manage one Joule instance instead of multiple isolated instances

This blog post will guide you through the step-by-step process of activating multi-system support for Private
Cloud Edition, complete with best practices to optimize your setup and ensure your users get the most out of
this powerful capability.
 

Architecture Overview

The architecture shows how a single Joule instance connects to multiple SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud systems through SAP Build Work Zone and BTP destinations, enabling unified access across your entire system landscape.

How Multi-System Joule Works

Multi-system Joule fundamentally changes how the digital assistant interacts with your system landscape:

System Awareness
Joule capabilities become system-aware, meaning they're visible and available based on specific conditions, primarily user authorizations and system access. When a user opens Joule, they see scenarios relevant to the systems they're authorized to execute.

Intelligent System Selection
When you submit a prompt to Joule, the AI analyzes several factors to determine the appropriate target system:
  1. Your user authorizations across different systems
  2. System descriptions and characteristics configured in the Joule Admin Center
  3. Keywords and context within your prompt
  4. Previously selected systems within the current conversation
Single-System Execution
While Joule can connect to multiple systems, each individual scenario executes in exactly one system at a time. This ensures data consistency and clear transactional boundaries. However, you can run multiple scenarios across different systems within the same conversation.

Disambiguation Flow
If Joule can't confidently determine the target system from your prompt, it will ask you to select the appropriate system. Your selection is remembered for the conversation context, reducing the need for repeated clarifications.
 

Prerequisites

Before you begin setting up multi-system support, ensure you have the following in place:
  • Existing Joule Setup: Joule must already be configured and operational following the Joule Onboarding Guide
  • First S/4HANA System / Client: At least one SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud system already integrated with Joule via the standard integration process
  • Other S/4HANA Systems / Clients: Prepare the integration of other systems / clients by following the integration steps outlined in the Integration Guide for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition. Please Skip number 8 (Run the Joule Booster) and the creation of the SSO Destination (part of step 9) as these steps will be covered during this blog.
  • Admin Roles: Joule Tenant Admin roles assigned to your user for accessing the Joule Admin Center
  • BTP Access: Administrator access to your SAP BTP Cockpit for destination management
  • SAP Build Work Zone Access: Access to SAP Build Work Zone (standard or advanced edition) for managing content providers and channels

Step-by-Step Activation Process

Step 1: Configure BTP Destinations

The foundation of multi-system Joule is proper BTP destination configuration. Each S/4HANA system requires its own set of destinations, and since BTP doesn't allow duplicate destination names, you must use a clear naming convention.

Understanding the Naming Convention

Pattern: {DESTINATION_BASE_NAME}_{SYSTEM_ID}{SYSTEM_CLIENT}
Examples:
Original default: S4HANA_PCE_SSO
For system XXX123: S4HANA_PCE_SSO_XXX123
For system YYY789: S4HANA_PCE_SSO_YYY789

This naming pattern ensures destinations are:
Unique: Prevents naming conflicts in BTP
Identifiable: Administrators can immediately see which system a destination serves
Consistent: Follows a predictable pattern across all systems
 
! Make sure {SYSTEM_ID}does not include any special characters (./,;:\#) or white spaces !

Rename Your Existing Default Destination

  1. Navigate to your SAP BTP Cockpit → Your Subaccount → Connectivity → Destinations
  2. Locate the default destination created during your initial Joule setup: S4HANA_PCE_SSO
  3. Click the destination name to open its configuration
  4. Click Edit
  5. Update the Name field to include your system ID as a suffix
  6. Example: If your system is XXX with client 123, rename to S4HANA_PCE_SSO_XXX123
  7. Leave all other configuration settings unchanged
  8. Click Save
Screenshot of the new Destination Name

Create Destinations for Additional Systems

For each additional S/4HANA Private Cloud system you want to connect:
  1. Follow the standard destination configuration process for S/4HANA Private Cloud integration
  2. Apply the naming convention with the new system's ID
  3. Example: S4HANA_PCE_SSO_YYY789
  4. Configure all required destination properties (URL, authentication, etc.) to point to the new system
PropertyDescription/Value
NameS4HANA_PCE_SSO_{SYSTEM_ID}{SYSTEM_CLIENT}
TypeHTTP
DescriptionEnter a description.
URLThe URL of the Virtual Host of your Cloud Connector: 
https://<host>:<port>
Proxy TypeOnPremise
AuthenticationPrincipal Propagation
sap-client (additional property)
The client number of the ABAP system.
 
Critical Warning: Do NOT re-run the Joule Booster for additional systems. The Booster should only be used for initial Joule setup. For multi-system configuration, manually create destinations following the documented process.
 

Step 2: Include Systems in Joule Formation

Once your BTP destinations are configured, you need to register the additional S/4HANA systems in the Joule Formation.

Add Systems to Formation

  1. Navigate to your SAP BTP Cockpit → Your Global Account Overview → System Landscape: Formations
  2. Locate your Joule Formation
  3. In the Formation view, click the Include Systems button
  4. The "Include Systems in Formation" wizard opens

Configure Integration Details

On the Integration Details screen:
  1. From SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition [SYSTEM_ID] to Joule
  2. Select the new system you want to add
  3. Enable "Enable Capability Deployment"
  4. This checkbox ensures that Joule scenarios for this system are properly deployed and available
  5. Enable "Enable Joule in the Integrated System"
  6. This checkbox activates the Joule plugin in the SAP Fiori Launchpad for this system
  7. Click Review → Finish

Repeat for Additional Systems

If you have more than one additional S/4HANA system to connect, repeat the above steps for each system.

Joule Formation on BTP for Multi-System Configuration

Step 3: Map Custom System Names

This step establishes the critical link between how SAP Build Work Zone identifies your systems (CDM Content Provider ID) and how Joule refers to them (System Name). This mapping enables Joule to route scenario executions to the correct backend system.

Understanding the Mapping Concept

CDM Content Provider ID: The identifier used in SAP Build Work Zone to represent your S/4HANA system content
System Name: The identifier Joule uses internally to route requests to the correct system

In some cases, these identifiers may differ. The Custom System Name mapping ensures they're properly aligned so Joule can execute scenarios in the correct system.

Get CDM Provider Details from SAP Build Work Zone

  1. Navigate to your SAP BTP Cockpit → Your Subaccount → Instances and Subscriptions
  2. Click on your SAP Build Work Zone subscription to open the application
  3. In the left navigation panel, click Channel Manager
  4. The "Content Channels" view displays all registered content providers
  5. Locate each of your S/4HANA Private Cloud systems in the list
  6. Note the ID value for each system. You'll use these in the next step
Example: You might see entries like:
  • System XXX123 with ID and client: XXX123
  • System YYY789 with ID and client: YYY789
  • Or potentially technical IDs like: sap.start.6c746d76-2

Overview of the Channel Manager Configuration in SAP Build WorkZone

Update Custom System Name in Joule Admin Center

  1. Access Joule Admin Center
  2. Navigate to your Joule Admin Center URL (typically provided during Joule setup or available through the Joule Admin Center documentation)
  3. Select the sap_digital_assistant from the List of Assistants and navigate to the Detail View of your assistant
  4. Click Edit in the top-right corner
  5. Select System Name from the configuration options
  6. Select System Type
  7. In the "Select System Type" dialog, choose sap.s4pce
  8. This filters the view to show only your S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition systems
  9. Enter Custom System Names
  10. For each system listed, enter the Content Channel ID you noted from SAP Build Work Zone
  11. The mapping format is: [Default System Name] → [Custom System Name] (= Content Channel ID)
    Example:
    Default System Name: XXX123→ Custom System Name: XXX123
    Default System Name: YYY789 → Custom System Name: YYY789
  12. Click Save to persist your system name mappings
  13. Click again Edit in the top-right corner
  14. Select System Details
  15. Add a System Title and a meaningful System Description that provides context about the usage of the system
  16. Click Save to persist your system system descriptions

Overview of the System Configuration in the Joule Admin CenterEnsure Default and Custom Destination Mapping

Make sure that the names of your Default Destinations fit to the Destination names you configured in Step 1:

  1. Click Edit in the top-right corner
  2. Select System Name from the configuration options
  3. Verify if the Default Destination name fits to the Destination name you configured in Step 1 (Configure BTP Destinations):
    {DESTINATION_BASE_NAME}_{SYSTEM_ID_A}{SYSTEM_CLIENT_A}
    {DESTINATION_BASE_NAME}_{SYSTEM_ID_B}{SYSTEM_CLIENT_B}
  4. If the Default Destination differs from your configured name, use the Custom Destination column to map your system to the correct Destination name
Overview of the Destination Mapping in the Joule Admin Center

 Important: Fill in values for ALL system entries. Do not leave any entries empty, as this will cause routing failures.


Upon successful save, you'll see the updated mappings displayed in the Joule Admin Center UI, confirming that Joule can now correctly route requests to your S/4HANA systems.

Best Practices for Multi-System Success

The configuration steps above will get multi-system Joule up and running, but following these best practices will significantly improve the user experience and system selection accuracy.

System Naming and Descriptions

The system descriptions you configure in the Joule Admin Center directly influence how well Joule can determine the correct target system. These descriptions are analyzed as part of the AI's decision-making process.

Poor System Description

Finance System

Good System Description

Finance System for EMEA, handling accounts payable and receivable
for sub-brand AutoParts Europe with headquarters in Frankfurt, Germany.
Primary company code: 1010. Used by finance teams in Germany, France,
and United Kingdom.

Why it matters: The detailed description provides context clues that Joule can match against user prompts. Keywords like "EMEA," "Frankfurt," "Germany," "France," and "company code 1010" help Joule route requests accurately.

What to Include in System Descriptions

  • Geographic scope: Regions, countries, or cities the system serves
  • Organizational scope: Subsidiaries, brands, or business units
  • Functional scope: Primary business processes (finance, procurement, sales, etc.)
  • Key identifiers: Company codes, plants, sales organizations
  • User groups: Which teams or departments primarily use this system

User Prompt Best Practices

Educate your end users on how to write effective prompts for multi-system environments.

Vague Prompt

"Show my expenses"
Joule may need to ask which system you're referring to, adding extra steps to the interaction.

Specific Prompt

"Show my expenses for plant 1200 in Frankfurt"
The prompt includes contextual information (plant, location) that Joule can match against system descriptions for automatic routing.

Additional Tips for Users

  • Include location references when relevant ("in Germany," "for the US region")
  • Mention organizational units if applicable ("for the AutoParts division")
  • Reference system identifiers if known ("in QM7002," "company code 1010")
  • Build on conversation context: If you've already specified a system in the conversation, subsequent prompts can be less specific

Role Assignment Strategy

When assigning roles in SAP Build Work Zone, strive for distinct role profiles per system when possible. This helps Joule make more confident system selection decisions based on a user's authorizations.

Example Strategy:
System XXX123 (EMEA Finance): Assign role SAP_BR_AP_ACCOUNTANT
System YYY789 (NA Operations): Assign role SAP_BR_PURCHASING_AGENT

If a user has entirely distinct roles across systems, Joule can more easily determine intent. However, this isn't always feasible, as many users need the same functional roles across multiple systems. In such cases, the system descriptions and user prompt quality become even more important.
 

How System Disambiguation Works

Understanding Joule's system selection logic can help you optimize your configuration and train your users effectively.

Decision Factors

When you submit a prompt, Joule analyzes multiple factors simultaneously:
  1. User Authorizations: Which systems can you access? Which roles do you have in each system?
  2. System Descriptions: Keywords and phrases in the system descriptions configured in Joule Admin Center
  3. Prompt Content: Keywords, locations, identifiers, and context in your natural language prompt
  4. Conversation History: If you've already selected a system in this conversation, Joule considers that context

The Disambiguation Flow

User submits prompt
    ↓
Joule analyzes all decision factors
    ↓
Confidence level HIGH → Execute scenario in determined system
    ↓
Confidence level LOW → Prompt user to select system
    ↓
User makes selection → Selection remembered for conversation
    ↓
Subsequent prompts in conversation benefit from context

When Joule Asks You to Choose

If Joule cannot confidently determine the target system, it will present you with a system selection prompt. This typically happens when:
  • Your prompt is ambiguous and could apply to multiple systems
  • You have identical roles in multiple systems
  • System descriptions don't provide distinguishing characteristics
  • You're starting a new conversation without system context

Example Conversation

Having multiple SAP S/4HANA private cloud systems connected, an example conversation could look like the following, where the business user has equal role assignments in both connected systems. If the initial prompt does not provide an indicator for a preferred system, Joule will prompt the user to select the system:

Example Conversation of the Mullti-System Disambiguation

Limitations

  • With this release, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition now supports multi-system connectivity. However, the connection of multiple tenants from other SAP solutions e.g. SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, SAP Ariba, SAP Concur, SAP SuccessFactors etc. are currently not supported.
  • SAP Build WorkZone has a limit of 1000 assigned roles to a user. With connecting multiple system, customers need to ensure that this number won't be exceeded.

Conclusion

Multi-system support transforms Joule from a single-system assistant into a true enterprise-wide digital companion. By connecting multiple SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud systems to a single Joule instance, you provide your users with seamless access to AI-powered assistance across their entire system landscape.

Additional Ressources

SAP Help Documentation

entry.title: Re: Customer reference list in VA01 is shown but entries...
entry.content:
Dear @Dirk,

Based on the described behavior this does not look like a VA01 sales configuration issue or an issue with the Customer Reference field itself. The symptom matches a known in SAP KBA 3763555

SAP indicates that the issue is planned to be fixed with release 2608. Customers on Public Cloud 2602 should use the workaround provided in the SAP KBA until the upgrade to 2608. One of the temporary workarounds referenced by SAP is to roll back Chrome / Edge to version 148

Recommended approach:

  1. Confirm whether users are using Google Chrome / Microsoft Edge 149 or higher
  2. Apply the workaround from SAP KBA 3763555 until the system is upgraded to 2608
  3. Retest the VA01 Customer Reference history dropdown after the upgrade to 2608

More details below:

SAP KBA 3763555 – WEBGUI: History not working after Chrome / Edge 149 update

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Work Zone as UI Integration Layer: A Navigation Paradigm Powered by Exposure V2
entry.content:
Thank you for sharing these insights. While this might be a technological advancement and for sure has its benefits, we have thrown user empathy, out of the window. User experience is of paramount importance and the systems they use should be designed to make their life easier, not difficult be adding more steps to their processes.

There is no navigation back to the launchpad from which they opened the app. Imagine a rep who makes 1000+ calls a day and uses the apps for almost every single call. Now they have to deal with remembering to close 1000 new tabs with no way of navigating back. And if they don't, they now have several sessions of the launchpad open. This is confusing and overwhelming for the users. Might as well remove the back navigation button and the logo click option to take the users to the home page, as they don't really serve any purpose anymore.

Please share your insights on the below questions.

1. "There is no option for administrators or end users to change this behavior to in-place launching." Can this be added to the roadmap so that administrators can have the option to change this behavior? 

2. Is V2 exposure mandatory for Joule in BWZ or does Joule work flawlessly with V1 exposure too?

3. Is V2 exposure mandatory in general for BWZ, going forward or can customers choose to be on V1?
